Problem Statement\n    66→\n    67→The current roadmap CLI pipeline has a spec-fidelity gate that uses an LLM to\n    68→compare a spec against a generated roadmap and report deviations. This gate is\n    69→non-deterministic and gets stuck in infinite remediation loops.\n    70→\n    71→After 4 failed runs on the v3.0 release, five failure modes were identified:\n    72→\n    73→1. **Severity drift** — MEDIUM→HIGH across runs with no anchoring\n    74→2. **Full document regeneration** — destroying prior fixes\n    75→3. **Attention degradation** — on large inline-embedded documents\n    76→4. **No convergence mechanism** — no memory of prior findings\n    77→5. **Goalpost movement** — severity criteria re-interpreted each run\n    78→\n    79→### 1.1 Evidence\n    80→\n    81→| Evidence | Source | Impact |\n    82→|----------|--------|--------|\n    83→| Runs 1→3 showed gradual convergence (3→1 HIGH), then Run 4 regressed catastrophically | `fidelity-remediation-log.md` | All prior fixes lost |\n    84→| Severity classifications drift between runs on identical content | Run logs, vote files | Non-deterministic gate behavior |\n    85→| Full roadmap regenerated from scratch on Run 4 | `fidelity-remediation-log.md` | Phase structure, numbering, paths all changed |\n    86→| Phantom requirement IDs (FR-009..FR-038) fabricated by LLM | `vote-2.md`, `vote-4.md` | False deviations inflate severity counts |\n    87→| Gate only checks `high_severity_count == 0` in frontmatter | `gates.py` | No validation of report quality or classification accuracy |\n    88→\n    89→### 1.2 Scope Boundary\n    90→\n    91→**In scope**: Refactoring the fidelity comparison, severity classification,\n    92→deviation tracking, remediation editing, and convergence control subsystems.\n    93→Budget enforcement for the convergence loop via TurnLedger (imported from\n    94→sprint/models.py as a pure-data economic primitive). The convergence engine\n    95→receives a pre-allocated TurnLedger instance from the pipeline executor; it\n    96→does not construct its own.\n    97→\n    98→**Out of scope**: Upstream pipeline steps (extract, generate, diff, debate,\n    99→score, merge). Test strategy generation. Certification step. TurnLedger class\n   100→modifications (consumed as-is). TurnLedger migration to pipeline/models.py\n   101→(future cleanup). Sprint-level budget wiring (execute_sprint,\n   102→execute_phase_tasks). Gate-pass reimbursement activation in the sprint loop\n   103→(v3.1 scope). Convergence-specific tracking (run counting, monotonic progress)\n   104→is handled by the convergence loop and DeviationRegistry, not by a separate\n   105→budget dataclass.\n   106→\n   107→**Preserved and coexisting**: `spec_patch.py` (the accepted-deviation workflow)\n   108→operates in both legacy and convergence modes. In legacy mode, it patches\n   109→spec text before fidelity comparison. In convergence mode, its `spec_hash`\n   110→interaction with FR-6 (registry reset on spec change) is preserved. v3.05\n   111→does not modify spec_patch.py; it coexists with the new fidelity subsystem.\n   112→\n   113→### 1.3 v3.0 Baseline\n   114→\n   115→This spec was originally drafted before v3.0 shipped. During v3.0 implementation,\n   116→~60% of the v3.05 infrastructure was pre-built. The spec MUST be read as\n   117→\"extend existing code,\" not \"build from scratch.\" The following inventory is\n   118→verified against commit `f4d9035`.\n   119→\n   120→#### Pre-Existing Modules scoring (RubricScores, score_argument, judge_verdict), wire_debate_verdict(), prosecutor/defender templates | FR-4, FR-4.1, FR-4.2, FR-5 |\n   126→| `remediate_executor.py` | 563 | Snapshot extraction |\n   159→| `structural_checkers.py` | FR-1 | 5 deterministic dimension checkers |\n   160→\n   161→#### Dead Code (DELETE)\n   162→\n   163→| Module | Evidence |\n   164→|--------|----------|\n   165→| `fidelity.py` (66 lines) | Zero imports across codebase; superseded by Finding + DeviationRegistry |\n   166→\n   167→**Reading convention**: In all subsequent FR sections, acceptance criteria marked\n   168→`[x]` with a source reference are v3.0 baseline items (verify, do not rebuild).\n   169→Unchecked `[ ]` items require implementation.\n   170→\n   171→---\n   172→\n   173→## 2. Clarified User Goals\n   174→\n   175→G1. **Determinism**: Same spec + same roadmap → same findings and severity\n   176→    classifications, every time.\n   177→\n   178→G2. **Convergence**: The pipeline MUST terminate. 3 runs max (catch → verify →\n   179→    backup). If it can't converge in 3, halt and report — don't loop.\n   180→\n   181→G3. **Edit preservation**: Remediation never destroys prior fixes. No full\n   182→    document regeneration without explicit user consent.\n   183→\n   184→G4. **Anchored severity**: Severity is determined by structural rules where\n   185→    possible (~70% of deviations). When LLM assigns HIGH, adversarial debate\n   186→    validates it before the pipeline acts on it.\n   187→\n   188→G5. **Scalability**: Large specs and roadmaps handled via chunked/sectional\n   189→    comparison, not full-inline embedding.\n   190→\n   191→G6. **Auditability**: Every finding, every severity assignment, every\n   192→    remediation edit is traceable to a rule or debate verdict.\n   193→\n   194→---\n   195→\n   196→## 3. Functional Requirements\n   197→\n   198→### FR-1: Decomposed Structural Checkers (5 Dimensions)\n   199→\n   200→**Description**: Replace the monolithic LLM fidelity comparison with 5\n   201→independent, statically-typed checkers — one per dimension. Each checker\n   202→extracts structured data from spec and roadmap, compares deterministically,\n   203→and produces typed findings with predetermined severity rules.\n   204→\n   205→**Checkers**:\n   206→\n   207→| # | Checker | Extracts From Spec | Extracts From Roadmap | Structural % |\n   208→|---|---------|--------------------|-----------------------|-------------|\n   209→| 1 | **Signatures** | Function sigs from fenced Python, FR/NFR/SC ID sets | Referenced IDs, function names/params in task descriptions | 80% |\n   210→| 2 | **Data Models** | File manifest tables (Sec 4.1/4.2), dataclass fields, enum literals | File paths in tasks, field references, mode coverage | 85% |\n   211→| 3 | **Gates** | `GateCriteria` fields, `Step(...)` params, semantic check names, step ordering | Gate implementation tasks, parameter coverage | 65% |\n   212→| 4 | **CLI Options** | CLI table (Sec 5.1), config fields, `Literal[...]` modes, defaults | Config/option coverage in tasks | 75% |\n   213→| 5 | **NFRs** | Numeric thresholds, security primitives, dependency rules (Sec 6) | Corresponding targets/validations in roadmap | 55% |\n   214→\n   215→**Acceptance Criteria**:\n   216→- [ ] Each checker is an independent callable that takes (spec_path, roadmap_path) → List[Finding]\n   217→- [ ] Each checker produces findings with severity assigned by structural rules, not LLM prose\n   218→- [ ] Checkers can run in parallel (no shared state)\n   219→- [ ] Each finding includes: dimension, rule_id, severity, spec_quote, roadmap_quote_or_MISSING, location\n   220→- [ ] A checker registry maps dimension names to checker callables\n   221→\n   222→**Dependencies**: FR-2 (spec parser), FR-3 (severity rules)\n   223→\n   224→---\n   225→\n   226→### FR-2: Spec & Roadmap Parser\n   227→\n   228→**Description**: A parser that extracts structured data from the spec template\n   229→format (YAML frontmatter, markdown tables, fenced code blocks, heading\n   230→hierarchy, requirement ID patterns) and from roadmap markdown.\n   231→\n   232→**Acceptance Criteria**:\n   233→- [ ] Parses YAML frontmatter from both spec and roadmap\n   234→- [ ] Extracts markdown tables by section (keyed by heading path)\n   235→- [ ] Extracts fenced code blocks with language annotation\n   236→- [ ] Extracts requirement ID families via regex: `FR-\\d+\\.\\d+`, `NFR-\\d+\\.\\d+`, `SC-\\d+`, `G-\\d+`, `D\\d+`\n   237→- [ ] Extracts Python function signatures from fenced blocks: `def name(params) -> return_type`\n   238→- [ ] Extracts file paths from manifest tables (Sec 4.1, 4.2, 4.3)\n   239→- [ ] Extracts `Literal[...]` enum values from code blocks\n   240→- [ ] Extracts numeric threshold expressions: `< 5s`, `>= 90%`, `minimum 20`\n   241→- [ ] Returns structured objects, not raw text\n   242→- [ ] Graceful degradation on malformed YAML frontmatter: returns partial parse + `ParseWarning`\n   243→- [ ] Graceful degradation on irregular markdown tables (missing columns, extra pipes): best-effort extraction + `ParseWarning`\n   244→- [ ] Graceful degradation on missing/wrong language tags in fenced blocks: extracts content as raw text + `ParseWarning`\n   245→- [ ] `ParseWarning` list collected per parse call, surfaced to caller (not silently swallowed)\n   246→- [ ] Validated against real spec (`deterministic-fidelity-gate-requirements.md`) — not just synthetic test fixtures\n   247→\n   248→**Critical Path Note**: FR-2 is the entry point for the structural checker pipeline:\n   249→FR-2 (parser) → FR-1 (checkers) → FR-4 (semantic layer) → FR-7 (convergence gate).\n   250→A parser failure cascades to all downstream FRs. Robustness against real-world spec\n   251→variations is not optional.\n   252→\n   253→**Dependencies**: None. FR-1, FR-4, FR-5, FR-7 all depend on FR-2.\n   254→\n   255→---\n   256→\n   257→### FR-3: Anchored Severity Rules\n   258→\n   259→**Description**: Each structural checker has a severity rule table that maps\n   260→specific structural mismatch types to fixed severity levels. Severity is NOT\n   261→LLM-judged for structural findings.\n   262→\n   263→**Severity Rule Table** (canonical machine keys):\n   264→\n   265→| Dimension | Machine Key | Human Description | Severity |\n   266→|-----------|------------|-------------------|----------|\n   267→| Signatures | `phantom_id` | Roadmap references ID not in spec | HIGH |\n   268→| Signatures | `function_missing` | Function in spec missing from roadmap | HIGH |\n   269→| Signatures | `param_arity_mismatch` | Parameter count differs between spec and roadmap | MEDIUM |\n   270→| Signatures | `param_type_mismatch` | Parameter type differs between spec and roadmap | MEDIUM |\n   271→| Data Models | `file_missing` | Required spec file missing from roadmap | HIGH |\n   272→| Data Models | `path_prefix_mismatch` | File path prefix differs (e.g., `audit/` vs `cli/audit/`) | HIGH |\n   273→| Data Models | `enum_uncovered` | Enum literal from spec not covered in roadmap | MEDIUM |\n   274→| Data Models | `field_missing` | Dataclass/model field from spec not referenced in roadmap | MEDIUM |\n   275→| Gates | `frontmatter_field_missing` | Required frontmatter field not covered | HIGH |\n   276→| Gates | `step_param_missing` | Step parameter from spec missing in roadmap | MEDIUM |\n   277→| Gates | `ordering_violated` | Step ordering constraint violated | MEDIUM |\n   278→| Gates | `semantic_check_missing` | Named semantic check not mapped to task | MEDIUM |\n   279→| CLI | `mode_uncovered` | Config mode from spec not covered | MEDIUM |\n   280→| CLI | `default_mismatch` | Default value differs between spec and roadmap | MEDIUM |\n   281→| NFRs | `threshold_contradicted` | Numeric threshold contradicted | HIGH |\n   282→| NFRs | `security_missing` | Security primitive missing | HIGH |\n   283→| NFRs | `dep_direction_violated` | Dependency direction violated | HIGH |\n   284→| NFRs | `coverage_mismatch` | Coverage threshold mismatch | MEDIUM |\n   285→| NFRs | `dep_rule_missing` | Dependency rule from spec not addressed | MEDIUM |\n   286→\n   287→**Implementation Contract**:\n   288→\n   289→```python\n   290→# Canonical severity rules: dict keyed by (dimension, machine_key) → severity\n   291→SEVERITY_RULES: dict[tuple[str, str], str] = {\n   292→    (\"signatures\", \"phantom_id\"): \"HIGH\",\n   293→    (\"signatures\", \"function_missing\"): \"HIGH\",\n   294→    # ... all rows from table above\n   295→}\n   296→\n   297→def get_severity(dimension: str, mismatch_type: str) -> str:\n   298→    \"\"\"Look up severity for a given dimension + mismatch type.\n   299→\n   300→    Returns severity string. Raises KeyError for unknown combinations\n   301→    (forces explicit rule addition, not silent defaults).\n   302→    \"\"\"\n   303→```\n   304→\n   305→**Baseline**: No severity rule infrastructure exists in v3.0. This is genuinely new\n   306→code (CREATE). The `compute_stable_id()` function in `convergence.py` uses\n   307→`mismatch_type` as a hash input but does not define the rule table.\n   308→\n   309→**Acceptance Criteria**:\n   310→- [ ] Every structural checker has an explicit rule table\n   311→- [ ] Rules are defined in code (not prompt text), keyed by `(dimension, mismatch_type) → severity`\n   312→- [ ] Machine keys (`phantom_id`, `function_missing`, etc.) are used in code — not prose descriptions\n   313→- [ ] Same inputs always produce the same severity — no randomness, no LLM interpretation\n   314→- [ ] Rule table is extensible (new rules can be added without changing checker logic)\n   315→- [ ] Unknown `(dimension, mismatch_type)` combinations raise `KeyError` — no silent defaults\n   316→- [ ] `SEVERITY_RULES` dict and `get_severity()` function are the canonical API\n   317→- [ ] Finding objects use machine keys in `mismatch_type` field (see FR-6 ISS-024 for field extension)\n   318→\n   319→**Dependencies**: FR-1. ISS-024 (Finding dataclass `rule_id` field extension).\n   320→\n   321→---\n   322→\n   323→### FR-4: Residual Semantic Layer with Adversarial Validation\n   324→\n   325→**Description**: After structural checkers complete, a residual LLM pass\n   326→handles the ~30% of checks that require semantic judgment (prose sufficiency,\n   327→spec contradiction resolution, additive-but-benign assessment). When the\n   328→semantic layer assigns HIGH severity, it does NOT auto-accept. Instead, it\n   329→triggers a lightweight adversarial debate to validate the rating.\n   330→\n   331→**Existing baseline**: `semantic_layer.py` (337 lines) already implements prompt\n   332→budget constants and enforcement (FR-4.2), `build_semantic_prompt()` with\n   333→proportional budget allocation, debate scoring infrastructure (`RubricScores`,\n   334→`score_argument()`, `judge_verdict()`), and `wire_debate_verdict()`. v3.05 adds\n   335→`validate_semantic_high()` (orchestrator for FR-4.1 debate protocol) and\n   336→`run_semantic_layer()` (entry point for the residual semantic pass).\n   337→\n   338→**Semantic/structural boundary**: Structural checkers declare their\n   339→`mismatch_types` via their rule tables. Anything NOT in any checker's rule\n   340→table is \"uncovered\" and belongs to the semantic layer.\n   341→\n   342→**Acceptance Criteria**:\n   343→- [ ] Semantic layer receives only dimensions/aspects not covered by structural checkers\n   344→- [ ] Semantic layer uses chunked input (per-section, not full-document inline)\n   345→- [ ] When semantic layer assigns HIGH: pipeline pauses, spawns adversarial debate\n   346→- [ ] Adversarial debate produces a verdict: CONFIRM_HIGH, DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM, or DOWNGRADE_TO_LOW\n   347→- [ ] Verdict is recorded in the deviation registry with debate transcript reference\n   348→- [ ] Semantic MEDIUM and LOW findings are accepted without debate\n   349→- [ ] Semantic layer prompt includes the structural findings as context (to avoid re-checking what's already checked)\n   350→- [ ] All semantic findings are tagged with `source_layer=\"semantic\"` (see FR-6)\n   351→\n   352→#### FR-4.1: Lightweight Debate Protocol\n   353→\n   354→The adversarial debate for semantic HIGH validation uses a single-round\n   355→prosecutor/defender model with a deterministic automated judge. This is the\n   356→**lightweight** variant; the full `/sc:adversarial` protocol is reserved for\n   357→FR-8 regression validation.\n   358→\n   359→**Roles**:\n   360→\n   361→| Role | Type | LLM Calls | Purpose |\n   362→|------|------|-----------|---------|\n   363→| Prosecutor | ClaudeProcess | 1 | Argues finding IS correctly HIGH |\n   364→| Defender | ClaudeProcess | 1 | Argues finding should be downgraded |\n   365→| Judge | Deterministic Python | 0 | Scores both against rubric, produces verdict |\n   366→\n   367→**Protocol**:\n   368→1. Build prosecutor prompt (argues HIGH) and defender prompt (argues downgrade)\n   369→2. Execute both via ClaudeProcess in **parallel** (2 LLM calls)\n   370→3. Parse YAML responses from each\n   371→4. Score both against 4-criterion rubric (deterministic Python, no LLM)\n   372→5. Compute margin = prosecutor_weighted − defender_weighted\n   373→6. Verdict: margin > 0.15 → CONFIRM_HIGH; margin < −0.15 → DOWNGRADE; else → CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative tiebreak)\n   374→7. Write debate output YAML to `output_dir/debates/{finding.stable_id}/debate.yaml`\n   375→\n   376→**Orchestrator Function**:\n   377→\n   378→```python\n   379→def validate_semantic_high(\n   380→    finding: Finding,\n   381→    registry: DeviationRegistry,\n   382→    output_dir: Path,\n   383→    *,\n   384→    claude_process_factory: Callable | None = None,\n   385→) -> str:\n   386→    \"\"\"Orchestrate lightweight adversarial debate for a semantic HIGH finding.\n   387→\n   388→    Implements FR-4.1 protocol steps 1-7:\n   389→    1. Build prosecutor + defender prompts from finding context\n   390→    2. Execute both via ClaudeProcess in parallel (2 LLM calls)\n   391→    3. Parse YAML responses; default scores to 0 on parse failure\n   392→    4. Score both via score_argument() against 4-criterion rubric\n   393→    5. Compute verdict via judge_verdict()\n   394→    6. Write debate YAML to output_dir/debates/{stable_id}/debate.yaml\n   395→    7. Call wire_debate_verdict() to update registry\n   396→\n   397→    Args:\n   398→        finding: The semantic HIGH finding to validate.\n   399→        registry: Active deviation registry for verdict recording.\n   400→        output_dir: Directory for debate output artifacts."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01MEUKLtGn9kZWHKyudXULDY","session_id":"2c1d6ebb-19ec-47d3-acbc-ab9ea0b88919","uuid":"546f830a-03b5-469f-81cc-2333ecfbbfb4","timestamp":"2026-03-20T20:35:34.156Z"}

`/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/fidelity.py`\n\nThat matters for phase 4: phase 4 is not inventing the core architecture, it is building on an already-delivered parser/checker/registry foundation.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 1 delivered: parser, sectioning, data model extensions, interface verification\n\nPrimary files:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/phase-1-tasklist.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0001/evidence.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0002/spec.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0004/evidence.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0005/spec.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0007/spec.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0010/evidence.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0011/evidence.md`\n\n### Phase 1 scope\nPhase 1 established:\n- interface contract verification against existing code\n- spec/roadmap parser\n- section splitting and dimension routing\n- data model extensions needed by downstream checkers/registry\n- parser tests and real-spec validation\n- Gate A certification\n\n### What was built\n\n#### A. Interface verification\nD-0001 confirms these foundations were checked before implementation:\n\n- TurnLedger API surface matches spec:\n  - `debit()`\n  - `credit()`\n  - `can_launch()`\n  - `can_remediate()`\n  - `reimbursement_rate`\n- `DeviationRegistry` public surface in `convergence.py` was documented\n- `convergence_enabled` defaults to `False`\n- `fidelity.py` is dead code with zero imports\n- `handle_regression()` did not exist yet and was expected to be added later\n- SC-1 through SC-6 were mapped to validation methods\n\nKey implication for phase 4:\nthe expected integration surfaces were already pinned down; phase 4 should not reinterpret those APIs.\n\n#### B. Parser module\nD-0002 documents the parser API in\n`/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/spec_parser.py`\n\nPublic dataclasses:\n- `ParseWarning`\n- `CodeBlock`\n- `TableRow`\n- `MarkdownTable`\n- `FunctionSignature`\n- `ThresholdExpression`\n- `SpecSection`\n- `ParseResult`\n\nPublic functions:\n- `parse_document`\n- `parse_frontmatter`\n- `extract_tables`\n- `extract_code_blocks`\n- `extract_requirement_ids`\n- `extract_function_signatures`\n- `extract_literal_values`\n- `extract_thresholds`\n- `extract_file_paths`\n- `extract_file_paths_from_tables`\n- `split_into_sections`\n\nThis is a substantial foundational decision: later phases are meant to consume structured parser output, not raw regex over whole documents.\n\n#### C. Real-spec validation\nD-0004 shows the parser was validated against the actual release spec:\n`/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/deterministic-fidelity-gate-requirements.md`\n\nValidation counts:\n- 12 frontmatter keys\n- 24 tables\n- 13 code blocks\n- 17 FR IDs\n- 7 NFR IDs\n- 1 G ID\n- 5 function signatures\n- 11 thresholds\n- 50 sections\n\nWarnings:\n- 5 code-block warnings for missing language tags\n\nArchitectural takeaway:\nthe parser is explicitly intended to degrade gracefully with warnings, not fail hard. Phase 4 should preserve that behavior.\n\n#### D. Section splitting and dimension routing\nD-0005 defines the sectioning model:\n- `SpecSection(heading, heading_path, level, content, start_line, end_line)`\n- frontmatter and preamble are treated as level-0 sections\n- round-trip reassembly is byte-for-byte\n\nDimension mapping:\n- signatures -> FR-1, FR-2, functional requirements\n- data_models -> sections 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, file manifest\n- gates -> FR-7, FR-8, 5.1\n- cli -> 5.1, CLI, Commands\n- nfrs -> section 6 / NFR / Non-Functional\n\nThis is important for phase 4 because semantic or orchestration work should use this section-local architecture rather than full-document inline comparisons.\n\n#### E. Data model extensions\nD-0007 says `Finding` was extended with:\n- `rule_id`\n- `spec_quote`\n- `roadmap_quote`\n- `stable_id`\n\nAnd supporting types added/documented:\n- `RegressionResult`\n- `RemediationPatch`\n- `ParseWarning`\n- `RunMetadata`\n\nThis means downstream phases are supposed to work with typed findings carrying traceable evidence.\n\n#### F. Exit criterion was SC-1 determinism proof.\n\n### What was built\n\n#### A. Checker interface and registry\nD-0012 defines:\n- `CheckerCallable = Callable[[str, str], list[Finding]]`\n- `CHECKER_REGISTRY` with 5 entries:\n  - `signatures`\n  - `data_models`\n  - `gates`\n  - `cli`\n  - `nfrs`\n\nEach checker:\n- reads spec and roadmap\n- parses via `parse_document()`\n- routes to relevant sections\n- compares deterministically\n- returns `Finding` objects with severity from `get_severity()`\n\nArchitecturally, this makes phase 4 integration target obvious: orchestration should dispatch via registry, not bespoke per-checker wiring.\n\n#### B. Signatures and data models checkers\nD-0013 documents:\n\nSignatures checker keys:\n- `phantom_id` HIGH\n- `function_missing` HIGH\n- `param_arity_mismatch` MEDIUM\n- `param_type_mismatch` MEDIUM\n\nData models checker keys:\n- `file_missing` HIGH\n- `path_prefix_mismatch` HIGH\n- `enum_uncovered` MEDIUM\n- `field_missing` MEDIUM\n\nThese checkers use parser outputs, not LLM judgment.\n\n#### C. Gates, CLI, and NFR checkers\nD-0014 documents:\n\nGates keys:\n- `frontmatter_field_missing` HIGH\n- `step_param_missing` MEDIUM\n- `ordering_violated` MEDIUM\n- `semantic_check_missing` MEDIUM\n\nCLI keys:\n- `mode_uncovered` MEDIUM\n- `default_mismatch` MEDIUM\n\nNFR keys:\n- `threshold_contradicted` HIGH\n- `security_missing` HIGH\n- `dep_direction_violated` HIGH\n- `coverage_mismatch` MEDIUM\n- `dep_rule_missing` MEDIUM\n\nThis completed the planned five-dimension deterministic layer.\n\n#### D. Canonical severity rule table\nD-0015 confirms `SEVERITY_RULES` has 19 entries and `get_severity()` raises `KeyError` for unknown combos.\n\nNotable discrepancy:\n- Tasklist/spec language in some places says 7 HIGH / 12 MEDIUM.\n- D-0015 evidence says 8 HIGH / 11 MEDIUM.\n- The table shown in D-0015 enumerates 8 HIGHs:\n  - 2 signatures\n  - 2 data_models\n  - 1 gates\n  - 0 cli\n  - 3 nfrs\n\nSo the actual delivered rule table appears to be 8 HIGH / 11 MEDIUM.\n\nThat inconsistency is worth keeping in mind for phase 4 if any logic assumes a particular distribution.\n\n#### E. Determinism proof\nD-0016 certifies:\n- sequential determinism\n- identical serialized output\n- per-checker determinism\n- parallel determinism via thread pool\n- no shared mutable state\n\nDeterminism guarantees were attributed to:\n- pure functions\n- sorted output by `(dimension, rule_id, location)`\n- deterministic `stable_id`\n- no shared mutable state\n\nPhase 2 conclusion:\nBy the end of phase 2, fidelity’s structural core had been decomposed into deterministic, typed, parallel-safe checkers with rule-anchored severities.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 3 delivered: DeviationRegistry upgrades, stable IDs, backward compatibility, run memory\n\nPrimary files:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/phase-3-tasklist.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0017/spec.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0018/spec.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0019/evidence.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0020/spec.md`\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/.dev/releases/current/v3.05_DeterministicFidelityGates/artifacts/D-0021/evidence.md`\n\n### Phase 3 scope\nMake the registry the durable memory system for convergence:\n- source-layer tracking\n- stable identity across runs\n- FIXED transitions\n- backward compatibility\n- prior findings memory\n\n### What was built\n\n#### A. Registry extensions and stable IDs\nD-0017 says `DeviationRegistry` now tracks:\n- `source_layer`: `\"structural\"` or `\"semantic\"`\n- stable IDs computed from:\n  - `dimension`\n  - `rule_id`\n  - `spec_location`\n  - `mismatch_type`\n\nStable ID algorithm:\n- SHA-256 of those properties, truncated to 16 hex chars\n\nCross-run behavior:\n- same stable ID -> update existing finding\n- missing on later run -> mark `FIXED`\n- new stable ID -> create new finding\n\nThis is the core persistence contract that phase 4 must respect.\n\n#### B. RunMetadata split counts\nD-0018 defines `RunMetadata` with:\n- `run_number`\n- `timestamp`\n- `spec_hash`\n- `roadmap_hash`\n- `structural_high_count`\n- `semantic_high_count`\n- `total_high_count`\n- plus `medium_count`, `low_count`\n\nArchitectural significance:\nphase 4 and later convergence logic are supposed to reason on split structural vs semantic HIGHs, not a single undifferentiated count.\n\n#### C. Spec version detection and pre-v3.05 compatibility\nD-0019 confirms:\n- `spec_hash` mismatch resets the registry\n- older registries missing `source_layer` default it to `\"structural\"`\n- `first_seen_run` and `last_seen_run` default to `1` on legacy data\n- status `\"ACTIVE\"` is accepted\n- serialization preserves new fields\n\nThis means phase 4 must preserve migration-safe registry reads/writes.\n\n#### D. Run-to-run memory\nD-0020 documents:\n- each finding tracks `first_seen_run` and `last_seen_run`\n- `get_prior_findings_summary(max_entries=50)` returns oldest-first summary\n- fixed findings that reappear reactivate using the same stable ID\n- no duplicate entries should be created for resurfacing issues\n\nThis is a key architectural context for phase 4: later remediation and semantic steps are expected to consume prior-finding summaries from the registry, not rediscover context from scratch.\n\n#### E. 3-run simulation\nD-0021 validates lifecycle behavior across three runs:\n- run 1 seeds findings\n- run 2 fixes one and adds one\n- run 3 confirms cumulative state and FIXED transitions\n- stable IDs remain unique and consistent\n- split counts stay correct\n\nPhase 3 conclusion:\nBy the end of phase 3, the convergence memory substrate existed and had been certified independently of the eventual orchestration loop.\n\n---\n\n## Architectural summary of phases 1-3\n\nIf you are building phase 4, the prior work implies this architecture is already established:\n\n### Delivered foundation\n1. Parser layer\n   - parses real spec/roadmap documents into structured data\n   - tolerant of imperfect formatting\n   - supports section-based routing\n\n2. Structural checker layer\n   - 5 checker families\n   - deterministic, parallel-safe\n   - machine-key findings only\n   - severity from code, not prompts\n\n3. Typed finding/model layer\n   - `Finding` enriched with `rule_id`, quotes, and `stable_id`\n   - supporting types for regression/remediation/run metadata\n\n4. Registry/memory layer\n   - stable cross-run identity\n   - source-layer separation\n   - FIXED lifecycle\n   - spec-hash reset\n   - prior-finding summary\n\n### What phases 1-3 did not yet deliver\nThey intentionally stopped short of the full convergence orchestration and semantic/remediation integration. Based on the tasklists and spec, those are phase 4+ concerns:\n- semantic-layer integration on top of structural findings\n- regression handling orchestration\n- convergence loop behavior\n- debate/remediation coupling\n- budgeted run control\n\n## Most important takeaways for phase 4\n\n1. Do not rebuild parser/checker foundations.\n   Those are already treated as delivered and certified.\n\n2. _truncate_to_budget(content: str, budget_bytes: int) -> str:\n   146→    \"\"\"Tail-truncate content to fit within byte budget on line boundaries.\n   147→\n   148→    BF-7 resolution: proportional budget allocation with tail-truncation.\n   149→    \"\"\"\n   150→    encoded = content.encode(\"utf-8\")\n   151→    if len(encoded) <= budget_bytes:\n   152→        return content\n   153→\n   154→    omitted = len(encoded) - budget_bytes\n   155→    marker = TRUNCATION_MARKER.format(omitted)\n   156→    marker_bytes = len(marker.encode(\"utf-8\")) + 1  # +1 for newline\n   157→    target = budget_bytes - marker_bytes\n   158→\n   159→    if target <= 0:\n   160→        return marker\n   161→\n   162→    # Truncate on line boundary\n   163→    truncated = encoded[:target].decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\")\n   164→    last_newline = truncated.rfind(\"\\n\")\n   165→    if last_newline > 0:\n   166→        truncated = truncated[:last_newline]\n   167→\n   168→    return truncated + \"\\n\" + marker\n   169→\n   170→\n   171→def build_semantic_prompt(request: SemanticCheckRequest) -> str:\n   172→    \"\"\"Build a chunked prompt for one Tiebreak: conservative -- uncertain cases stay HIGH\n   308→        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n   309→\n   310→\n   311→# --- Debate-Registry Wiring (Task 4.3) ---\n   312→\n   313→def wire_debate_verdict(\n   314→    registry: Any,  # 30KB hard limit\n\n# Proportional allocation (BF-7 resolution)\nBUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT = 0.60     # 60% = 18,432 bytes\nBUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT = 0.20   # 20% = 6,144 bytes\nBUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT = 0.15    # 15% = 4,608 bytes\nBUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT = 0.05         # 5%  = 1,536 bytes\n\nTRUNCATION_MARKER = \"[TRUNCATED: {} bytes omitted]\"\n\n\n# --- Debate Protocol Constants (BF-6 resolution) ---\n\nDEBATE_TOKEN_CAP = 5_000  # max tokens per finding debate\n\n# Rubric weights for deterministic judge\nRUBRIC_WEIGHTS = {\n    \"evidence_quality\": 0.30,\n    \"impact_specificity\": 0.25,\n    \"logical_coherence\": 0.25,\n    \"concession_handling\": 0.20,\n}\n\nVERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD = 0.15  # margin > 0.15 = clear winner\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Scores for one side of the debate (prosecutor or defender).\"\"\"\n    evidence_quality: int = 0      # 0-3\n    impact_specificity: int = 0    # 0-3\n    logical_coherence: int = 0     # 0-3\n    concession_handling: int = 0   # 0-3\n\n    @property\n    def weighted_score(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Compute weighted score normalized to [0.0, 1.0].\"\"\"\n        raw = (\n            self.evidence_quality * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"evidence_quality\"]\n            + self.impact_specificity * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"impact_specificity\"]\n            + self.logical_coherence * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"logical_coherence\"]\n            + self.concession_handling * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"concession_handling\"]\n        )\n        return raw / 3.0  # Normalize: max raw per criterion is 3\n\n\n# --- Prompt Templates ---\n\nPROSECUTOR_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the PROSECUTOR in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding IS correctly classified as HIGH severity.\nProvide concrete evidence from the spec and roadmap.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: HIGH\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\nDEFENDER_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the DEFENDER in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding should be DOWNGRADED from HIGH.\nIf downgrading, recommend MEDIUM or LOW with justification.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: <MEDIUM or LOW>\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\n\n# --- Semantic Check Request ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass SemanticCheckRequest:\n    \"\"\"Input to the semantic LLM layer for one dimension.\"\"\"\n    dimension: str\n    spec_sections: list[Any]       # SpecSection objects\n    roadmap_sections: list[Any]    # SpecSection objects\n    structural_findings: list[Finding]\n    prior_findings_summary: str\n\n\n# --- Core Functions ---\n\ndef _truncate_to_budget(content: str, budget_bytes: int) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Tail-truncate content to fit within byte budget on line boundaries.\n\n    BF-7 resolution: proportional budget allocation with tail-truncation.\n    \"\"\"\n    encoded = content.encode(\"utf-8\")\n    if len(encoded) <= budget_bytes:\n        return content\n\n    omitted = len(encoded) - budget_bytes\n    marker = TRUNCATION_MARKER.format(omitted)\n    marker_bytes = len(marker.encode(\"utf-8\")) + 1  # +1 for newline\n    target = budget_bytes - marker_bytes\n\n    if target <= 0:\n        return marker\n\n    # Truncate on line boundary\n    truncated = encoded[:target].decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\")\n    last_newline = truncated.rfind(\"\\n\")\n    if last_newline > 0:\n        truncated = truncated[:last_newline]\n\n    return truncated + \"\\n\" + marker\n\n\ndef build_semantic_prompt(request: SemanticCheckRequest) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build a chunked prompt for one semantic check.\n\n    Enforces NFR-3: prompt size <= 30KB via proportional budget\n    allocation (BF-7 resolution).\n    \"\"\"\n    template_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT)\n    spec_roadmap_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT)\n    structural_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT)\n    prior_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT)\n\n    # Build template\n    template = (\n        f\"## Semantic Fidelity Check: {request.dimension}\\n\\n\"\n        \"Analyze the following spec and roadmap sections for semantic \"\n        \"deviations NOT already covered by structural checkers.\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Already Checked (do NOT re-report)\\n\"\n    )\n\n    template_bytes = len(template.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    if template_bytes > template_budget:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"Template overhead ({template_bytes}B) exceeds budget ({template_budget}B)\"\n        )\n\n    # Truncate spec+roadmap sections\n    spec_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.spec_sections\n    )\n    roadmap_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.roadmap_sections\n    )\n    combined = f\"### Spec Sections\\n{spec_text}\\n\\n### Roadmap Sections\\n{roadmap_text}\"\n    combined = _truncate_to_budget(combined, spec_roadmap_budget)\n\n    # Truncate structural findings context\n    structural_text = \"\\n\".join(\n        f\"- [{f.severity}] {f.dimension}: {f.description}\"\n        for f in request.structural_findings\n    )\n    structural_text = _truncate_to_budget(structural_text, structural_budget)\n\n    # Truncate prior findings summary\n    prior_text = _truncate_to_budget(request.prior_findings_summary, prior_budget)\n\n    prompt = (\n        f\"{template}\"\n        f\"{structural_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"### Prior Findings (for anchoring)\\n{prior_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"{combined}\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Instructions\\n\"\n        \"Report only NEW semantic deviations. Assign severity: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.\\n\"\n        \"For each finding, provide: dimension, description, severity, evidence.\\n\"\n    )\n\n    # Final enforcement assert\n    prompt_size = len(prompt.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    assert prompt_size <= MAX_PROMPT_BYTES, (\n        f\"Prompt size {prompt_size}B exceeds {MAX_PROMPT_BYTES}B budget\"\n    )\n\n    return prompt\n\n\ndef score_argument(response_yaml: dict) -> RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Score a debate argument using the deterministic rubric.\n\n    BF-6 resolution: automated judge with 4-criterion rubric.\n    Scores 0-3 per criterion based on evidence in the response.\n    \"\"\"\n    evidence_points = response_yaml.get(\"evidence_points\", [])\n    argument = response_yaml.get(\"argument\", \"\")\n    confidence = response_yaml.get(\"confidence\", 0.0)\n\n    # Evidence quality: count of concrete evidence points\n    eq = min(len(evidence_points), 3)\n\n    # Impact specificity: argument length and specificity\n    word_count = len(argument.split())\n    if word_count >= 100:\n        imp = 3\n    elif word_count >= 50:\n        imp = 2\n    elif word_count >= 20:\n        imp = 1\n    else:\n        imp = 0\n\n    # Logical coherence: confidence level maps to coherence\n    if confidence >= 0.8:\n        lc = 3\n    elif confidence >= 0.6:\n        lc = 2\n    elif confidence >= 0.4:\n        lc = 1\n    else:\n        lc = 0\n\n    # Concession handling: presence of caveats/qualifications\n    concession_markers = [\"however\", \"although\", \"while\", \"despite\", \"caveat\", \"limitation\"]\n    concessions = sum(1 for m in concession_markers if m in argument.lower())\n    ch = min(concessions, 3)\n\n    return RubricScores(\n        evidence_quality=eq,\n        impact_specificity=imp,\n        logical_coherence=lc,\n        concession_handling=ch,\n    )\n\n\ndef judge_verdict(\n    prosecutor_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_recommendation: str = \"MEDIUM\",\n) -> tuple[str, float]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic judge: compare weighted scores, apply margin threshold.\n\n    BF-6 resolution:\n    - Prosecutor margin > 0.15 -> CONFIRM_HIGH\n    - Defender margin > 0.15 -> DOWNGRADE_TO_{recommendation}\n    - Margin <= 0.15 (tiebreak) -> CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative)\n\n    Returns (verdict, margin).\n    \"\"\"\n    p_score = prosecutor_scores.weighted_score\n    d_score = defender_scores.weighted_score\n    margin = p_score - d_score\n\n    if margin > VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n    elif margin < -VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return f\"DOWNGRADE_TO_{defender_recommendation}\", margin\n    else:\n        # Tiebreak: conservative -- uncertain cases stay HIGH\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n\n\n# --- Debate-Registry Wiring (Task 4.3) ---\n\ndef wire_debate_verdict(\n    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry from convergence module\n    finding: Finding,\n    verdict: str,\n    transcript_path: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Wire debate verdict into the deviation registry.\n\n    After validate_semantic_high() returns, this updates the registry\n    with the debate outcome and adjusts severity if downgraded.\n    \"\"\"\n    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '')\n    if not stable_id:\n        logger.warning(\"Finding has no stable_id; cannot record verdict\")\n        return\n\n    registry.record_debate_verdict(stable_id, verdict, transcript_path)\n\n    if verdict.startswith(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\"):\n        new_severity = verdict.replace(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\", \"\")\n        logger.info(\n            \"Finding %s downgraded from HIGH to %s by debate verdict\",\n            stable_id[:8], new_severity,\n        )\n","numLines":337,"startLine":1,"totalLines":337}}}

     dimension=\"signatures\",\n                            mismatch_type=\"param_type_mismatch\",\n                            description=f\"Function '{sig.name}' param {i} type differs: check_nfrs(spec_path: str, roadmap_path: str) -> list[Finding]:\n    \"\"\"NFRs checker: verifies numeric thresholds, security primitives, dependency rules.\n\n    Machine keys:                    dimension=\"nfrs\",\n                    mismatch_type=\"threshold_contradicted\",\n                    description=f\"Threshold '{spec_t.raw}' contradicted by '{rm_t.raw}' in Not a regression.\",\n            prev_semantic, curr_semantic, curr_semantic - prev_semantic,\n        )\n\n    # Only structural increase triggers regression\n    if curr_structural > False\n\n\n_active_validation_dirs: list[Path] = []\n\n\ndef _create_validation_dirs(\n    spec_path: Path,\n    roadmap_path: Path,\n    registry_path: Path,\n    count: int = 3,\n) -> list[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Create temporary directories with independent file copies.\n\n    Each directory gets its own copy of spec, roadmap, and registry.\n    Replaces git worktrees (BF-4: checkers don't need git repo).\n    \"\"\"\n    dirs: list[Path] = []\n    for i in range(count):\n        d = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix=f\"fidelity-validation-{i}-\"))\n        shutil.copy2(spec_path, d / spec_path.name)\n        shutil.copy2(roadmap_path, d / roadmap_path.name)\n        if registry_path.exists():\n            shutil.copy2(registry_path, d / registry_path.name)\n        dirs.append(d)\n\n    _active_validation_dirs.extend(dirs)\n    return dirs\n\n\ndef _cleanup_validation_dirs(dirs: list[Path]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Remove temporary validation directories.\n\n    Best-effort cleanup: logs warning on failure, does not raise.\n    \"\"\"\n    for d in dirs:\n        try:\n            shutil.rmtree(d)\n            if d in _active_validation_dirs:\n                _active_validation_dirs.remove(d)\n        except OSError as exc:\n            logger.warning(\"Failed to clean up validation dir %s: %s\", d, exc)\n\n\ndef _atexit_cleanup() -> None:\n    \"\"\"Fallback cleanup registered with atexit.\"\"\"\n    if _active_validation_dirs:\n        logger.info(\"atexit: cleaning up %d validation dirs\", len(_active_validation_dirs))\n        _cleanup_validation_dirs(list(_active_validation_dirs))\n\n\natexit.register(_atexit_cleanup)\n","numLines":334,"startLine":1,"totalLines":334}}}

This gate is\n    69→non-deterministic and gets stuck in infinite remediation loops.\n    70→\n    71→After 4 failed runs on the v3.0 release, five failure modes were identified:\n    72→\n    73→1. **Severity drift** — MEDIUM→HIGH across runs with no anchoring\n    74→2. **Full document regeneration** — destroying prior fixes\n    75→3. **Attention degradation** — on large inline-embedded documents\n    76→4. **No convergence mechanism** — no memory of prior findings\n    77→5. **Goalpost movement** — severity criteria re-interpreted each run\n    78→\n    79→### 1.1 Evidence\n    80→\n    81→| Evidence | Source | Impact |\n    82→|----------|--------|--------|\n    83→| Runs 1→3 showed gradual convergence (3→1 HIGH), then Run 4 regressed catastrophically | `fidelity-remediation-log.md` | All prior fixes lost |\n    84→| Severity classifications drift between runs on identical content | Run logs, vote files | Non-deterministic gate behavior |\n    85→| Full roadmap regenerated from scratch on Run 4 | `fidelity-remediation-log.md` | Phase structure, numbering, paths all changed |\n    86→| Phantom requirement IDs (FR-009..FR-038) fabricated by LLM | `vote-2.md`, `vote-4.md` | False deviations inflate severity counts |\n    87→| Gate only checks `high_severity_count == 0` in frontmatter | `gates.py` | No validation of report quality or classification accuracy |\n    88→\n    89→### 1.2 Scope Boundary\n    90→\n    91→**In scope**: Refactoring the fidelity comparison, severity classification,\n    92→deviation tracking, remediation editing, and convergence control subsystems.\n    93→Budget enforcement for the convergence loop via TurnLedger (imported from\n    94→sprint/models.py as a pure-data economic primitive). Problem Statement\n\nThe current roadmap CLI pipeline has a spec-fidelity gate that uses an LLM to\ncompare a spec against a generated roadmap and report deviations. This gate is\nnon-deterministic and gets stuck in infinite remediation loops.\n\nAfter 4 failed runs on the v3.0 release, five failure modes were identified:\n\n1. **Severity drift** — MEDIUM→HIGH across runs with no anchoring\n2. **Full document regeneration** — destroying prior fixes\n3. **Attention degradation** — on large inline-embedded documents\n4. **No convergence mechanism** — no memory of prior findings\n5. **Goalpost movement** — severity criteria re-interpreted each run\n\n### 1.1 Evidence\n\n| Evidence | Source | Impact |\n|----------|--------|--------|\n| Runs 1→3 showed gradual convergence (3→1 HIGH), then Run 4 regressed catastrophically | `fidelity-remediation-log.md` | All prior fixes lost |\n| Severity classifications drift between runs on identical content | Run logs, vote files | Non-deterministic gate behavior |\n| Full roadmap regenerated from scratch on Run 4 | `fidelity-remediation-log.md` | Phase structure, numbering, paths all changed |\n| Phantom requirement IDs (FR-009..FR-038) fabricated by LLM | `vote-2.md`, `vote-4.md` | False deviations inflate severity counts |\n| Gate only checks `high_severity_count == 0` in frontmatter | `gates.py` | No validation of report quality or classification accuracy |\n\n### 1.2 Scope Boundary\n\n**In scope**: Refactoring the fidelity comparison, severity classification,\ndeviation tracking, remediation editing, and convergence control subsystems.\nBudget enforcement for the convergence loop via TurnLedger (imported from\nsprint/models.py as a pure-data economic primitive). The convergence engine\nreceives a pre-allocated TurnLedger instance from the pipeline executor; it\ndoes not construct its own.\n\n**Out of scope**: Upstream pipeline steps (extract, generate, diff, debate,\nscore, merge). Test strategy generation. Certification step. TurnLedger class\nmodifications (consumed as-is). TurnLedger migration to pipeline/models.py\n(future cleanup). Sprint-level budget wiring (execute_sprint,\nexecute_phase_tasks). Gate-pass reimbursement activation in the sprint loop\n(v3.1 scope). Convergence-specific tracking (run counting, monotonic progress)\nis handled by the convergence loop and DeviationRegistry, not by a separate\nbudget dataclass.\n\n**Preserved and coexisting**: `spec_patch.py` (the accepted-deviation workflow)\noperates in both legacy and convergence modes. In legacy mode, it patches\nspec text before fidelity comparison. In convergence mode, its `spec_hash`\ninteraction with FR-6 (registry reset on spec change) is preserved. v3.05\ndoes not modify spec_patch.py; it coexists with the new fidelity subsystem.\n\n### 1.3 v3.0 Baseline\n\nThis spec was originally drafted before v3.0 shipped. During v3.0 implementation,\n~60% of the v3.05 infrastructure was pre-built. The spec MUST be read as\n\"extend existing code,\" not \"build from scratch.\" The following inventory is\nverified against commit `f4d9035`.\n\n#### Pre-Existing Modules (MODIFY, not CREATE)\n\n| FR-8, FR-10 |\n| `semantic_layer.py` | 337 | Prompt budget constants + enforcement (FR-4.2), build_semantic_prompt(), debate scoring (RubricScores, score_argument, judge_verdict), wire_debate_verdict(), prosecutor/defender templates | FR-4, FR-4.1, FR-4.2, FR-5 |\n| `remediate_executor.py` | 563 | Snapshot create/restore/cleanup, enforce_allowlist(), parallel ClaudeProcess Clarified User Goals\n\nG1. **Determinism**: Same spec + same roadmap → same findings and severity\n    classifications, every time.\n\nG2. **Convergence**: The pipeline MUST terminate. 3 runs max (catch → verify →\n    backup). When the\n   328→semantic layer assigns HIGH severity, it does NOT auto-accept. Instead, it\n   329→triggers a lightweight adversarial debate to validate the rating.\n   330→\n   331→**Existing baseline**: `semantic_layer.py` (337 lines) already implements prompt\n   332→budget constants and enforcement (FR-4.2), `build_semantic_prompt()` with\n   333→proportional budget allocation, debate scoring infrastructure (`RubricScores`,\n   334→`score_argument()`, `judge_verdict()`), and `wire_debate_verdict()`. v3.05 adds\n   335→`validate_semantic_high()` (orchestrator for FR-4.1 debate protocol) and\n   336→`run_semantic_layer()` (entry point for the residual semantic pass).\n   337→\n   338→**Semantic/structural boundary**: Structural checkers declare their\n   339→`mismatch_types` via their rule tables. Anything NOT in any checker's rule\n   340→table is \"uncovered\" and belongs to the semantic layer.\n   341→\n   342→**Acceptance Criteria**:\n   343→- [ ] Semantic layer receives only dimensions/aspects not covered by structural checkers\n   344→- [ ] Semantic layer uses chunked input (per-section, not full-document inline)\n   345→- [ ] When semantic layer assigns HIGH: pipeline pauses, spawns adversarial debate\n   346→- [ ] Adversarial debate produces a verdict: CONFIRM_HIGH, DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM, or DOWNGRADE_TO_LOW\n   347→- [ ] Verdict is recorded in the deviation registry with debate transcript reference\n   348→- [ ] Semantic MEDIUM and LOW findings are accepted without debate\n   349→- [ ] Semantic layer prompt includes the structural findings as context (to avoid re-checking what's already checked)\n   350→- [ ] All semantic findings are tagged with `source_layer=\"semantic\"` (see FR-6)\n   351→\n   352→#### FR-4.1: Lightweight Debate Protocol\n   353→\n   354→The adversarial debate for semantic HIGH validation uses a single-round\n   355→prosecutor/defender model with a deterministic automated judge. This is the\n   356→**lightweight** variant; the full `/sc:adversarial` protocol is reserved for\n   357→FR-8 regression validation.\n   358→\n   359→**Roles**:\n   360→\n   361→| Role | Type | LLM Calls | Purpose |\n   362→|------|------|-----------|---------|\n   363→| Prosecutor | ClaudeProcess | 1 | Argues finding IS correctly HIGH |\n   364→| Defender | ClaudeProcess | 1 | Argues finding should be downgraded |\n   365→| Judge | Deterministic Python | 0 | Scores both against rubric, produces verdict |\n   366→\n   367→**Protocol**:\n   368→1. Build prosecutor prompt (argues HIGH) and defender prompt (argues downgrade)\n   369→2. Execute both via ClaudeProcess in **parallel** (2 LLM calls)\n   370→3. Parse YAML responses from each\n   371→4. Score both against 4-criterion rubric (deterministic Python, no LLM)\n   372→5. Compute margin = prosecutor_weighted − defender_weighted\n   373→6. Verdict: margin > 0.15 → CONFIRM_HIGH; margin < −0.15 → DOWNGRADE; else → CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative tiebreak)\n   374→7. Build prosecutor + defender prompts from finding context\n   390→    2. Execute both via ClaudeProcess in parallel (2 LLM calls)\n   391→    3. Parse YAML responses; default scores to 0 on parse failure\n   392→    4. Score both via score_argument() against 4-criterion rubric\n   393→    5. Compute verdict via judge_verdict()\n   394→    6. Write debate YAML to output_dir/debates/{stable_id}/debate.yaml\n   395→    7. all rows from table above\n}\n\ndef get_severity(dimension: str, mismatch_type: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Look up severity for a given dimension + mismatch type.\n\n    Returns severity string. Raises KeyError for unknown combinations\n    (forces explicit rule addition, not silent defaults).\n    \"\"\"\n```\n\n**Baseline**: No severity rule infrastructure exists in v3.0. The `compute_stable_id()` function in `convergence.py` uses\n`mismatch_type` as a hash input but does not define the rule table.\n\n**Acceptance Criteria**:\n- [ ] Every structural checker has an explicit rule table\n- [ ] Rules are defined in code (not prompt text), keyed by `(dimension, mismatch_type) → severity`\n- [ ] Machine keys (`phantom_id`, `function_missing`, etc.) are used in code — not prose descriptions\n- [ ] Same inputs always produce the same severity — no randomness, no LLM interpretation\n- [ ] Rule table is extensible (new rules can be added without changing checker logic)\n- [ ] Unknown `(dimension, mismatch_type)` combinations raise `KeyError` — no silent defaults\n- [ ] `SEVERITY_RULES` dict and `get_severity()` function are the canonical API\n- [ ] Finding objects use machine keys in `mismatch_type` field (see FR-6 ISS-024 for field extension)\n\n**Dependencies**: FR-1. ISS-024 (Finding dataclass `rule_id` field extension).\n\n---\n\n### FR-4: Residual Semantic Layer with Adversarial Validation\n\n**Description**: After structural checkers complete, a residual LLM pass\nhandles the ~30% of checks that require semantic judgment (prose sufficiency,\nspec contradiction resolution, additive-but-benign assessment). When the\nsemantic layer assigns HIGH severity, it does NOT auto-accept. Instead, it\ntriggers a lightweight adversarial debate to validate the rating.\n\n**Existing baseline**: `semantic_layer.py` (337 lines) already implements prompt\nbudget constants and enforcement (FR-4.2), `build_semantic_prompt()` with\nproportional budget allocation, debate scoring infrastructure (`RubricScores`,\n`score_argument()`, `judge_verdict()`), and `wire_debate_verdict()`. v3.05 adds\n`validate_semantic_high()` (orchestrator for FR-4.1 debate protocol) and\n`run_semantic_layer()` (entry point for the residual semantic pass).\n\n**Semantic/structural boundary**: Structural checkers declare their\n`mismatch_types` via their rule tables. Anything NOT in any checker's rule\ntable is \"uncovered\" and belongs to the semantic layer.\n\n**Acceptance Criteria**:\n- [ ] Semantic layer receives only dimensions/aspects not covered by structural checkers\n- [ ] Semantic layer uses chunked input (per-section, not full-document inline)\n- [ ] When semantic layer assigns HIGH: pipeline pauses, spawns adversarial debate\n- [ ] Adversarial debate produces a verdict: CONFIRM_HIGH, DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM, or DOWNGRADE_TO_LOW\n- [ ] Verdict is recorded in the deviation registry with debate transcript reference\n- [ ] Semantic MEDIUM and LOW findings are accepted without debate\n- [ ] Semantic layer prompt includes the structural findings as context (to avoid re-checking what's already checked)\n- [ ] All semantic findings are tagged with `source_layer=\"semantic\"` (see FR-6)\n\n#### FR-4.1: Lightweight Debate Protocol\n\nThe adversarial debate for semantic HIGH validation uses a single-round\nprosecutor/defender model with a deterministic automated judge. This is the\n**lightweight** variant; the full `/sc:adversarial` protocol is reserved for\nFR-8 regression validation.\n\n**Roles**:\n\n| Role | Type | LLM Calls | Purpose |\n|------|------|-----------|---------|\n| Prosecutor | ClaudeProcess | 1 | Argues finding IS correctly HIGH |\n| Defender | ClaudeProcess | 1 | Argues finding should be downgraded |\n| Judge | Deterministic Python | 0 | Scores both against rubric, produces verdict |\n\n**Protocol**:\n1. Build prosecutor prompt (argues HIGH) and defender prompt (argues downgrade)\n2. Execute both via ClaudeProcess in **parallel** (2 LLM calls)\n3. Parse YAML responses from each\n4. Score both against 4-criterion rubric (deterministic Python, no LLM)\n5. Compute margin = prosecutor_weighted − defender_weighted\n6. Verdict: margin > 0.15 → CONFIRM_HIGH; margin < −0.15 → DOWNGRADE; else → CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative tiebreak)\n7. Write debate output YAML to `output_dir/debates/{finding.stable_id}/debate.yaml`\n\n**Orchestrator Function**:\n\n```python\ndef validate_semantic_high(\n    finding: Finding,\n    registry: DeviationRegistry,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    *,\n    claude_process_factory: Callable | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Orchestrate lightweight adversarial debate for a semantic HIGH finding.\n\n    Implements FR-4.1 protocol steps 1-7:\n    1. Build prosecutor + defender prompts from finding context\n    2. Execute both via ClaudeProcess in parallel (2 LLM calls)\n    3. Parse YAML responses; default scores to 0 on parse failure\n    4. Score both via score_argument() against 4-criterion rubric\n    5. Compute verdict via judge_verdict()\n    6. Write debate YAML to output_dir/debates/{stable_id}/debate.yaml\n    7. Call wire_debate_verdict() to update registry\n\n    Args:\n        finding: The semantic HIGH finding to validate.\n        registry: Active deviation registry for verdict recording.","numLines":200,"startLine":200,"totalLines":1372}}}

29→\n    30→\n    31→# --- Debate Protocol Constants (BF-6 resolution) ---\n    32→\n    33→DEBATE_TOKEN_CAP = 5_000  # max tokens per finding debate\n    34→\n    35→# Rubric weights for \"concession_handling\": 0.20,\n    41→}\n    42→\n    43→VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD = 0.15  # margin > 0.15 = clear winner\n    44→\n    45→\n    46→@dataclass(frozen=True)\n    47→class RubricScores:\n    48→    \"\"\"Scores for one side of the debate (prosecutor or defender).\"\"\"\n    49→    evidence_quality: int = 0      # 0-3\n    50→    impact_specificity: int = 0    # 0-3\n    51→    logical_coherence: int = 0     # 0-3\n    52→    concession_handling: int = 0   # 0-3\n    53→\n    54→    @property\n    55→    def weighted_score(self) -> float:\n    56→        \"\"\"Compute weighted score normalized to [0.0, 1.0].\"\"\"\n    57→        raw = (\n    58→            self.evidence_quality * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"evidence_quality\"]\n    59→            + self.impact_specificity * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"impact_specificity\"]\n    60→            + self.logical_coherence * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"logical_coherence\"]\n    61→            + self.concession_handling * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"concession_handling\"]\n    62→        )\n    63→        return raw / 3.0  # Normalize: max raw per criterion is 3\n    64→\n    65→\n    66→# --- Prompt Templates ---\n    67→\n   79→{spec_context}\n    80→\n    81→## Roadmap Context\n    82→{roadmap_context}\n    83→\n    84→## Your Task\n    85→Argue that this finding IS correctly classified as HIGH severity.\n    86→Provide concrete evidence from the spec and roadmap.\n    87→\n    88→## Response Format (YAML)\n    89→```yaml\n    90→argument: |\n    91→  <your argument>\n    92→evidence_points:\n    93→  - <point 1>\n    94→  - <point 2>\n    95→recommended_severity: HIGH\n    96→confidence: <0.0-1.0>\n    97→```\"\"\"\n    98→\n    99→DEFENDER_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the DEFENDER in a severity validation debate.\n   100→\n   101→## Finding Under Review\n   102→- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n   103→- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n   104→- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n   105→- **Description**: {description}\n   106→- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n   107→- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n   108→\n   109→## Spec Context\n   110→{spec_context}\n   111→\n   112→## Roadmap Context\n   113→{roadmap_context}\n   114→\n   115→## Your Task\n   116→Argue that this finding should be DOWNGRADED from HIGH.\n   117→If downgrading, recommend MEDIUM or LOW with justification.\n   118→\n   119→## Response Format (YAML)\n   120→```yaml\n   121→argument: |\n   122→  <your argument>\n   123→evidence_points:\n   124→  - <point 1>\n   125→  - <point 2>\n   126→recommended_severity: <MEDIUM DeviationRegistry from convergence module\n   315→    finding: Finding,\n   316→    verdict: str,\n   317→    transcript_path: str,\n   318→) -> None:\n   319→    \"\"\"Wire debate verdict into the deviation registry.\n   320→\n   321→    After validate_semantic_high() returns, this updates the registry\n   322→    with the debate outcome and adjusts severity if downgraded.\n   323→    \"\"\"\n   324→    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '')\n   325→    if not stable_id:\n   326→        logger.warning(\"Finding has no stable_id; cannot record verdict\")\n   327→        return\n   328→\n   329→    registry.record_debate_verdict(stable_id, verdict, transcript_path)\n   330→\n   331→    if verdict.startswith(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\"):\n   332→        new_severity = verdict.replace(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\", \"\")\n   333→        logger.info(\n   334→            \"Finding %s downgraded from HIGH to %s by debate verdict\",\n   335→            stable_id[:8], new_severity,\n   336→        )\n   337→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2c1d6ebb-19ec-47d3-acbc-ab9ea0b88919","uuid":"e495be21-40a3-491a-b384-d50867d1f2ae","timestamp":"2026-03-20T20:39:29.181Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/semantic_layer.py","content":"\"\"\"Residual semantic layer with adversarial validation.\n\nImplements FR-4 (semantic checking), FR-5 (chunked comparison),\nand the lightweight debate protocol (BF-6 resolution).\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom .models import Finding\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# --- Prompt Budget Constants (BF-7 / NFR-3) ---\n\nMAX_PROMPT_BYTES = 30_720  # 30KB hard limit\n\n# Proportional allocation (BF-7 resolution)\nBUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT = 0.60     # 60% = 18,432 bytes\nBUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT = 0.20   # 20% = 6,144 bytes\nBUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT = 0.15    # 15% = 4,608 bytes\nBUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT = 0.05         # 5%  = 1,536 bytes\n\nTRUNCATION_MARKER = \"[TRUNCATED: {} bytes omitted]\"\n\n\n# --- Debate Protocol Constants (BF-6 resolution) ---\n\nDEBATE_TOKEN_CAP = 5_000  # max tokens per finding debate\n\n# Rubric weights for deterministic judge\nRUBRIC_WEIGHTS = {\n    \"evidence_quality\": 0.30,\n    \"impact_specificity\": 0.25,\n    \"logical_coherence\": 0.25,\n    \"concession_handling\": 0.20,\n}\n\nVERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD = 0.15  # margin > 0.15 = clear winner\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Scores for one side of the debate (prosecutor or defender).\"\"\"\n    evidence_quality: int = 0      # 0-3\n    impact_specificity: int = 0    # 0-3\n    logical_coherence: int = 0     # 0-3\n    concession_handling: int = 0   # 0-3\n\n    @property\n    def weighted_score(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Compute weighted score normalized to [0.0, 1.0].\"\"\"\n        raw = (\n            self.evidence_quality * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"evidence_quality\"]\n            + self.impact_specificity * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"impact_specificity\"]\n            + self.logical_coherence * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"logical_coherence\"]\n            + self.concession_handling * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"concession_handling\"]\n        )\n        return raw / 3.0  # Normalize: max raw per criterion is 3\n\n\n# --- Prompt Templates ---\n\nPROSECUTOR_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the PROSECUTOR in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding IS correctly classified as HIGH severity.\nProvide concrete evidence from the spec and roadmap.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: HIGH\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\nDEFENDER_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the DEFENDER in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding should be DOWNGRADED from HIGH.\nIf downgrading, recommend MEDIUM or LOW with justification.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: <MEDIUM or LOW>\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\n\n# --- Semantic Check Request ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass SemanticCheckRequest:\n    \"\"\"Input to the semantic LLM layer for one dimension.\"\"\"\n    dimension: str\n    spec_sections: list[Any]       # SpecSection objects\n    roadmap_sections: list[Any]    # SpecSection objects\n    structural_findings: list[Finding]\n    prior_findings_summary: str\n\n\n# --- Core Functions ---\n\ndef _truncate_to_budget(content: str, budget_bytes: int) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Tail-truncate content to fit within byte budget on line boundaries.\n\n    BF-7 resolution: proportional budget allocation with tail-truncation.\n    \"\"\"\n    encoded = content.encode(\"utf-8\")\n    if len(encoded) <= budget_bytes:\n        return content\n\n    omitted = len(encoded) - budget_bytes\n    marker = TRUNCATION_MARKER.format(omitted)\n    marker_bytes = len(marker.encode(\"utf-8\")) + 1  # +1 for newline\n    target = budget_bytes - marker_bytes\n\n    if target <= 0:\n        return marker\n\n    # Truncate on line boundary\n    truncated = encoded[:target].decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\")\n    last_newline = truncated.rfind(\"\\n\")\n    if last_newline > 0:\n        truncated = truncated[:last_newline]\n\n    return truncated + \"\\n\" + marker\n\n\ndef build_semantic_prompt(request: SemanticCheckRequest) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build a chunked prompt for one semantic check.\n\n    Enforces NFR-3: prompt size <= 30KB via proportional budget\n    allocation (BF-7 resolution).\n    \"\"\"\n    template_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT)\n    spec_roadmap_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT)\n    structural_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT)\n    prior_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT)\n\n    # Build template\n    template = (\n        f\"## Semantic Fidelity Check: {request.dimension}\\n\\n\"\n        \"Analyze the following spec and roadmap sections for semantic \"\n        \"deviations NOT already covered by structural checkers.\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Already Checked (do NOT re-report)\\n\"\n    )\n\n    template_bytes = len(template.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    if template_bytes > template_budget:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"Template overhead ({template_bytes}B) exceeds budget ({template_budget}B)\"\n        )\n\n    # Truncate spec+roadmap sections\n    spec_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.spec_sections\n    )\n    roadmap_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.roadmap_sections\n    )\n    combined = f\"### Spec Sections\\n{spec_text}\\n\\n### Roadmap Sections\\n{roadmap_text}\"\n    combined = _truncate_to_budget(combined, spec_roadmap_budget)\n\n    # Truncate structural findings context\n    structural_text = \"\\n\".join(\n        f\"- [{f.severity}] {f.dimension}: {f.description}\"\n        for f in request.structural_findings\n    )\n    structural_text = _truncate_to_budget(structural_text, structural_budget)\n\n    # Truncate prior findings summary\n    prior_text = _truncate_to_budget(request.prior_findings_summary, prior_budget)\n\n    prompt = (\n        f\"{template}\"\n        f\"{structural_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"### Prior Findings (for anchoring)\\n{prior_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"{combined}\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Instructions\\n\"\n        \"Report only NEW semantic deviations. Assign severity: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.\\n\"\n        \"For each finding, provide: dimension, description, severity, evidence.\\n\"\n    )\n\n    # Final enforcement assert\n    prompt_size = len(prompt.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    assert prompt_size <= MAX_PROMPT_BYTES, (\n        f\"Prompt size {prompt_size}B exceeds {MAX_PROMPT_BYTES}B budget\"\n    )\n\n    return prompt\n\n\ndef score_argument(response_yaml: dict) -> RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Score a debate argument using the deterministic rubric.\n\n    BF-6 resolution: automated judge with 4-criterion rubric.\n    Scores 0-3 per criterion based on evidence in the response.\n    \"\"\"\n    evidence_points = response_yaml.get(\"evidence_points\", [])\n    argument = response_yaml.get(\"argument\", \"\")\n    confidence = response_yaml.get(\"confidence\", 0.0)\n\n    # Evidence quality: count of concrete evidence points\n    eq = min(len(evidence_points), 3)\n\n    # Impact specificity: argument length and specificity\n    word_count = len(argument.split())\n    if word_count >= 100:\n        imp = 3\n    elif word_count >= 50:\n        imp = 2\n    elif word_count >= 20:\n        imp = 1\n    else:\n        imp = 0\n\n    # Logical coherence: confidence level maps to coherence\n    if confidence >= 0.8:\n        lc = 3\n    elif confidence >= 0.6:\n        lc = 2\n    elif confidence >= 0.4:\n        lc = 1\n    else:\n        lc = 0\n\n    # Concession handling: presence of caveats/qualifications\n    concession_markers = [\"however\", \"although\", \"while\", \"despite\", \"caveat\", \"limitation\"]\n    concessions = sum(1 for m in concession_markers if m in argument.lower())\n    ch = min(concessions, 3)\n\n    return RubricScores(\n        evidence_quality=eq,\n        impact_specificity=imp,\n        logical_coherence=lc,\n        concession_handling=ch,\n    )\n\n\ndef judge_verdict(\n    prosecutor_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_recommendation: str = \"MEDIUM\",\n) -> tuple[str, float]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic judge: compare weighted scores, apply margin threshold.\n\n    BF-6 resolution:\n    - Prosecutor margin > 0.15 -> CONFIRM_HIGH\n    - Defender margin > 0.15 -> DOWNGRADE_TO_{recommendation}\n    - Margin <= 0.15 (tiebreak) -> CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative)\n\n    Returns (verdict, margin).\n    \"\"\"\n    p_score = prosecutor_scores.weighted_score\n    d_score = defender_scores.weighted_score\n    margin = p_score - d_score\n\n    if margin > VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n    elif margin < -VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return f\"DOWNGRADE_TO_{defender_recommendation}\", margin\n    else:\n        # Tiebreak: conservative -- uncertain cases stay HIGH\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n\n\n# --- Debate-Registry Wiring (Task 4.3) ---\n\ndef wire_debate_verdict(\n    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry from convergence module\n    finding: Finding,\n    verdict: str,\n    transcript_path: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Wire debate verdict into the deviation registry.\n\n    After validate_semantic_high() returns, this updates the registry\n    with the debate outcome and adjusts severity if downgraded.\n    \"\"\"\n    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '')\n    if not stable_id:\n        logger.warning(\"Finding has no stable_id; cannot record verdict\")\n        return\n\n    registry.record_debate_verdict(stable_id, verdict, transcript_path)\n\n    if verdict.startswith(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\"):\n        new_severity = verdict.replace(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\", \"\")\n        logger.info(\n            \"Finding %s downgraded from HIGH to %s by debate verdict\",\n            stable_id[:8], new_severity,\n        )\n","numLines":337,"startLine":1,"totalLines":337}}}
