0-3\n    logical_coherence: int = 0     # 0-3\n    concession_handling: int = 0   # 0-3\n\n    @property\n    def weighted_score(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Compute weighted score normalized to [0.0, 1.0].\"\"\"\n        raw = (\n            self.evidence_quality * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"evidence_quality\"]\n            + self.impact_specificity * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"impact_specificity\"]\n            + self.logical_coherence * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"logical_coherence\"]\n            + self.concession_handling * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"concession_handling\"]\n        )\n        return raw / 3.0  # Normalize: max raw per criterion is 3\n\n\n# --- Prompt Templates ---\n\nPROSECUTOR_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the PROSECUTOR in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding IS correctly classified as HIGH severity.\nProvide concrete evidence from the spec and roadmap.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: HIGH\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\nDEFENDER_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the DEFENDER in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding should be DOWNGRADED from HIGH.\nIf downgrading, recommend MEDIUM or LOW with justification.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: <MEDIUM or LOW>\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\n\n# --- Semantic Check Request ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass SemanticCheckRequest:\n    \"\"\"Input to the semantic LLM layer for one dimension.\"\"\"\n    dimension: str\n    spec_sections: list[Any]       # SpecSection objects\n    roadmap_sections: list[Any]    # SpecSection objects\n    structural_findings: list[Finding]\n    prior_findings_summary: str\n\n\n# --- Core Functions ---\n\ndef _truncate_to_budget(content: str, budget_bytes: int) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Tail-truncate content to fit within byte budget on line boundaries.\n\n    BF-7 resolution: proportional budget allocation with tail-truncation.\n    \"\"\"\n    encoded = content.encode(\"utf-8\")\n    if len(encoded) <= budget_bytes:\n        return content\n\n    omitted = len(encoded) - budget_bytes\n    marker = TRUNCATION_MARKER.format(omitted)\n    marker_bytes = len(marker.encode(\"utf-8\")) + 1  # +1 for newline\n    target = budget_bytes - marker_bytes\n\n    if target <= 0:\n        return marker\n\n    # Truncate on line boundary\n    truncated = encoded[:target].decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\")\n    last_newline = truncated.rfind(\"\\n\")\n    if last_newline > 0:\n        truncated = truncated[:last_newline]\n\n    return truncated + \"\\n\" + marker\n\n\ndef build_semantic_prompt(request: SemanticCheckRequest) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build a chunked prompt for one semantic check.\n\n    Enforces NFR-3: prompt size <= 30KB via proportional budget\n    allocation (BF-7 resolution).\n    \"\"\"\n    template_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT)\n    spec_roadmap_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT)\n    structural_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT)\n    prior_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT)\n\n    # Build template\n    template = (\n        f\"## Semantic Fidelity Check: {request.dimension}\\n\\n\"\n        \"Analyze the following spec and roadmap sections for semantic \"\n        \"deviations NOT already covered by structural checkers.\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Already Checked (do NOT re-report)\\n\"\n    )\n\n    template_bytes = len(template.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    if template_bytes > template_budget:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"Template overhead ({template_bytes}B) exceeds budget ({template_budget}B)\"\n        )\n\n    # Truncate spec+roadmap sections\n    spec_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.spec_sections\n    )\n    roadmap_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.roadmap_sections\n    )\n    combined = f\"### Spec Sections\\n{spec_text}\\n\\n### Roadmap Sections\\n{roadmap_text}\"\n    combined = _truncate_to_budget(combined, spec_roadmap_budget)\n\n    # Truncate structural findings context\n    structural_text = \"\\n\".join(\n        f\"- [{f.severity}] {f.dimension}: {f.description}\"\n        for f in request.structural_findings\n    )\n    structural_text = _truncate_to_budget(structural_text, structural_budget)\n\n    # Truncate prior findings summary\n    prior_text = _truncate_to_budget(request.prior_findings_summary, prior_budget)\n\n    prompt = (\n        f\"{template}\"\n        f\"{structural_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"### Prior Findings (for anchoring)\\n{prior_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"{combined}\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Instructions\\n\"\n        \"Report only NEW semantic deviations. Assign severity: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.\\n\"\n        \"For each finding, provide: dimension, description, severity, evidence.\\n\"\n    )\n\n    # Final enforcement assert\n    prompt_size = len(prompt.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    assert prompt_size <= MAX_PROMPT_BYTES, (\n        f\"Prompt size {prompt_size}B exceeds {MAX_PROMPT_BYTES}B budget\"\n    )\n\n    return prompt\n\n\ndef score_argument(response_yaml: dict) -> RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Score a debate argument using the deterministic rubric.\n\n    BF-6 resolution: automated judge with 4-criterion rubric.\n    Scores 0-3 per criterion based on evidence in the response.\n    \"\"\"\n    evidence_points = response_yaml.get(\"evidence_points\", [])\n    argument = response_yaml.get(\"argument\", \"\")\n    confidence = response_yaml.get(\"confidence\", 0.0)\n\n    # Evidence quality: count of concrete evidence points\n    eq = min(len(evidence_points), 3)\n\n    # Impact specificity: argument length and specificity\n    word_count = len(argument.split())\n    if word_count >= 100:\n        imp = 3\n    elif word_count >= 50:\n        imp = 2\n    elif word_count >= 20:\n        imp = 1\n    else:\n        imp = 0\n\n    # Logical coherence: confidence level maps to coherence\n    if confidence >= 0.8:\n        lc = 3\n    elif confidence >= 0.6:\n        lc = 2\n    elif confidence >= 0.4:\n        lc = 1\n    else:\n        lc = 0\n\n    # Concession handling: presence of caveats/qualifications\n    concession_markers = [\"however\", \"although\", \"while\", \"despite\", \"caveat\", \"limitation\"]\n    concessions = sum(1 for m in concession_markers if m in argument.lower())\n    ch = min(concessions, 3)\n\n    return RubricScores(\n        evidence_quality=eq,\n        impact_specificity=imp,\n        logical_coherence=lc,\n        concession_handling=ch,\n    )\n\n\ndef judge_verdict(\n    prosecutor_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_recommendation: str = \"MEDIUM\",\n) -> tuple[str, float]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic judge: compare weighted scores, apply margin threshold.\n\n    BF-6 resolution:\n    - Prosecutor margin > 0.15 -> CONFIRM_HIGH\n    - Defender margin > 0.15 -> DOWNGRADE_TO_{recommendation}\n    - Margin <= 0.15 (tiebreak) -> CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative)\n\n    Returns (verdict, margin).\n    \"\"\"\n    p_score = prosecutor_scores.weighted_score\n    d_score = defender_scores.weighted_score\n    margin = p_score - d_score\n\n    if margin > VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n    elif margin < -VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return f\"DOWNGRADE_TO_{defender_recommendation}\", margin\n    else:\n        # Tiebreak: conservative -- uncertain cases stay HIGH\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n\n\n# --- Debate-Registry Wiring (Task 4.3) ---\n\ndef wire_debate_verdict(\n    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry from convergence module\n    finding: Finding,\n    verdict: str,\n    transcript_path: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Wire debate verdict into the deviation registry.\n\n    After validate_semantic_high() returns, this updates the registry\n    with the debate outcome and adjusts severity if downgraded.\n    \"\"\"\n    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '')\n    if not stable_id:\n        logger.warning(\"Finding has no stable_id; cannot record verdict\")\n        return\n\n    registry.record_debate_verdict(stable_id, verdict, transcript_path)\n\n    if verdict.startswith(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\"):\n        new_severity = verdict.replace(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\", \"\")\n        logger.info(\n            \"Finding %s downgraded from HIGH to %s by debate verdict\",\n            stable_id[:8], new_severity,\n        )\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":25,"oldLines":7,"newStart":25,"newLines":7,"lines":[" BUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT = 0.15    # Concession handling: presence of caveats/qualifications\n    concession_markers = [\"however\", \"although\", \"while\", \"despite\", \"caveat\", \"limitation\"]\n    concessions = sum(1 for m in concession_markers if m in argument.lower())\n    ch = min(concessions, 3)\n\n    return RubricScores(\n        evidence_quality=eq,\n        impact_specificity=imp,\n        logical_coherence=lc,\n        concession_handling=ch,\n    )\n\n\ndef judge_verdict(\n    prosecutor_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_recommendation: str = \"MEDIUM\",\n) -> tuple[str, float]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic judge: compare weighted scores, apply margin threshold.\n\n    BF-6 resolution:\n    - Prosecutor margin > 0.15 -> CONFIRM_HIGH\n    - Defender margin > 0.15 -> DOWNGRADE_TO_{recommendation}\n    - Margin <= 0.15 (tiebreak) -> CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative)\n\n    Returns (verdict, margin).\n    \"\"\"\n    p_score = prosecutor_scores.weighted_score\n    d_score = defender_scores.weighted_score\n    margin = p_score - d_score\n\n    if margin > VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n    elif margin < -VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return f\"DOWNGRADE_TO_{defender_recommendation}\", margin\n    else:\n        # Tiebreak: conservative -- uncertain cases stay HIGH\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n\n\n# --- Debate-Registry Wiring (Task 4.3) ---\n\ndef wire_debate_verdict(\n    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry from convergence module\n    finding: Finding,\n    verdict: str,\n    transcript_path: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Wire debate verdict into the deviation registry.\n\n    After validate_semantic_high() returns, this updates the registry\n    with the debate outcome and adjusts severity if downgraded.\n    \"\"\"\n    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '')\n    if not stable_id:\n        logger.warning(\"Finding has no stable_id; cannot record verdict\")\n        return\n\n    registry.record_debate_verdict(stable_id, verdict, transcript_path)\n\n    if verdict.startswith(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\"):\n        new_severity = verdict.replace(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\", \"\")\n        logger.info(\n            \"Finding %s downgraded from HIGH to %s by debate verdict\",\n            stable_id[:8], new_severity,\n        )\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":142,"oldLines":17,"newStart":142,"newLines":18,"lines":[" "," # 30KB hard limit\n\n# Proportional allocation (BF-7 resolution)\nBUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT = 0.60     # 60% = 18,432 bytes\nBUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT = 0.20   # 20% = 6,144 bytes\nBUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT = 0.15    # 15% = 4,608 bytes\nBUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT = 0.05         # 5%  = 1,536 bytes\n\nTRUNCATION_MARKER = \"[TRUNCATED: {} bytes omitted from '{}']\"\n\n\n# --- Debate Protocol Constants (BF-6 resolution) ---\n\nDEBATE_TOKEN_CAP = 5_000  # max tokens per finding debate\n\n# Rubric weights for deterministic judge\nRUBRIC_WEIGHTS = {\n    \"evidence_quality\": 0.30,\n    \"impact_specificity\": 0.25,\n    \"logical_coherence\": 0.25,\n    \"concession_handling\": 0.20,\n}\n\nVERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD = 0.15  # margin > 0.15 = clear winner\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Scores for one side of the debate (prosecutor or defender).\"\"\"\n    evidence_quality: int = 0      # 0-3\n    impact_specificity: int = 0    # 0-3\n    logical_coherence: int = 0     # 0-3\n    concession_handling: int = 0   # 0-3\n\n    @property\n    def weighted_score(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Compute weighted score normalized to [0.0, 1.0].\"\"\"\n        raw = (\n            self.evidence_quality * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"evidence_quality\"]\n            + self.impact_specificity * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"impact_specificity\"]\n            + self.logical_coherence * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"logical_coherence\"]\n            + self.concession_handling * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"concession_handling\"]\n        )\n        return raw / 3.0  # Normalize: max raw per criterion is 3\n\n\n# --- Prompt Templates ---\n\nPROSECUTOR_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the PROSECUTOR in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding IS correctly classified as HIGH severity.\nProvide concrete evidence from the spec and roadmap.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: HIGH\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\nDEFENDER_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the DEFENDER in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding should be DOWNGRADED from HIGH.\nIf downgrading, recommend MEDIUM or LOW with justification.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: <MEDIUM or LOW>\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\n\n# --- Semantic Check Request ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass SemanticCheckRequest:\n    \"\"\"Input to the semantic LLM layer for one dimension.\"\"\"\n    dimension: str\n    spec_sections: list[Any]       # SpecSection objects\n    roadmap_sections: list[Any]    # SpecSection objects\n    structural_findings: list[Finding]\n    prior_findings_summary: str\n\n\n# --- Core Functions ---\n\ndef _truncate_to_budget(content: str, budget_bytes: int, heading: str = \"content\") -> str:\n    \"\"\"Tail-truncate content to fit within byte budget on line boundaries.\n\n    BF-7 resolution: proportional budget allocation with tail-truncation.\n    FR-4.2: truncation markers include section heading.\n    \"\"\"\n    encoded = content.encode(\"utf-8\")\n    if len(encoded) <= budget_bytes:\n        return content\n\n    omitted = len(encoded) - budget_bytes\n    marker = TRUNCATION_MARKER.format(omitted, heading)\n    marker_bytes = len(marker.encode(\"utf-8\")) + 1  # +1 for newline\n    target = budget_bytes - marker_bytes\n\n    if target <= 0:\n        return marker\n\n    # Truncate on line boundary\n    truncated = encoded[:target].decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\")\n    last_newline = truncated.rfind(\"\\n\")\n    if last_newline > 0:\n        truncated = truncated[:last_newline]\n\n    return truncated + \"\\n\" + marker\n\n\ndef build_semantic_prompt(request: SemanticCheckRequest) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build a chunked prompt for one semantic check.\n\n    Enforces NFR-3: prompt size <= 30KB via proportional budget\n    allocation (BF-7 resolution).\n    \"\"\"\n    template_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT)\n    spec_roadmap_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT)\n    structural_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT)\n    prior_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT)\n\n    # Build template\n    template = (\n        f\"## Semantic Fidelity Check: {request.dimension}\\n\\n\"\n        \"Analyze the following spec and roadmap sections for semantic \"\n        \"deviations NOT already covered by structural checkers.\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Already Checked (do NOT re-report)\\n\"\n    )\n\n    template_bytes = len(template.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    if template_bytes > template_budget:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"Template overhead ({template_bytes}B) exceeds budget ({template_budget}B)\"\n        )\n\n    # Truncate spec+roadmap sections\n    spec_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.spec_sections\n    )\n    roadmap_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.roadmap_sections\n    )\n    combined = f\"### Spec Sections\\n{spec_text}\\n\\n### Roadmap Sections\\n{roadmap_text}\"\n    combined = _truncate_to_budget(combined, spec_roadmap_budget)\n\n    # Truncate structural findings context\n    structural_text = \"\\n\".join(\n        f\"- [{f.severity}] {f.dimension}: {f.description}\"\n        for f in request.structural_findings\n    )\n    structural_text = _truncate_to_budget(structural_text, structural_budget)\n\n    # Truncate prior findings summary\n    prior_text = _truncate_to_budget(request.prior_findings_summary, prior_budget)\n\n    prompt = (\n        f\"{template}\"\n        f\"{structural_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"### Prior Findings (for anchoring)\\n{prior_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"{combined}\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Instructions\\n\"\n        \"Report only NEW semantic deviations. Assign severity: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.\\n\"\n        \"For each finding, provide: dimension, description, severity, evidence.\\n\"\n    )\n\n    # Final enforcement assert\n    prompt_size = len(prompt.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    assert prompt_size <= MAX_PROMPT_BYTES, (\n        f\"Prompt size {prompt_size}B exceeds {MAX_PROMPT_BYTES}B budget\"\n    )\n\n    return prompt\n\n\ndef score_argument(response_yaml: dict) -> RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Score a debate argument using the deterministic rubric.\n\n    BF-6 resolution: automated judge with 4-criterion rubric.\n    Scores 0-3 per criterion based on evidence in the response.\n    \"\"\"\n    evidence_points = response_yaml.get(\"evidence_points\", [])\n    argument = response_yaml.get(\"argument\", \"\")\n    confidence = response_yaml.get(\"confidence\", 0.0)\n\n    # Evidence quality: count of concrete evidence points\n    eq = min(len(evidence_points), 3)\n\n    # Impact specificity: argument length and specificity\n    word_count = len(argument.split())\n    if word_count >= 100:\n        imp = 3\n    elif word_count >= 50:\n        imp = 2\n    elif word_count >= 20:\n        imp = 1\n    else:\n        imp = 0\n\n    # Logical coherence: confidence level maps to coherence\n    if confidence >= 0.8:\n        lc = 3\n    elif confidence >= 0.6:\n        lc = 2\n    elif confidence >= 0.4:\n        lc = 1\n    else:\n        lc = 0\n\n    # Concession handling: presence of caveats/qualifications\n    concession_markers = [\"however\", \"although\", \"while\", \"despite\", \"caveat\", \"limitation\"]\n    concessions = sum(1 for m in concession_markers if m in argument.lower())\n    ch = min(concessions, 3)\n\n    return RubricScores(\n        evidence_quality=eq,\n        impact_specificity=imp,\n        logical_coherence=lc,\n        concession_handling=ch,\n    )\n\n\ndef judge_verdict(\n    prosecutor_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_recommendation: str = \"MEDIUM\",\n) -> tuple[str, float]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic judge: compare weighted scores, apply margin threshold.\n\n    BF-6 resolution:\n    - Prosecutor margin > 0.15 -> CONFIRM_HIGH\n    - Defender margin > 0.15 -> DOWNGRADE_TO_{recommendation}\n    - Margin <= 0.15 (tiebreak) -> CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative)\n\n    Returns (verdict, margin).\n    \"\"\"\n    p_score = prosecutor_scores.weighted_score\n    d_score = defender_scores.weighted_score\n    margin = p_score - d_score\n\n    if margin > VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n    elif margin < -VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return f\"DOWNGRADE_TO_{defender_recommendation}\", margin\n    else:\n        # Tiebreak: conservative -- uncertain cases stay HIGH\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n\n\n# --- Debate-Registry Wiring (Task 4.3) ---\n\ndef wire_debate_verdict(\n    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry from convergence module\n    finding: Finding,\n    verdict: str,\n    transcript_path: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Wire debate verdict into the deviation registry.\n\n    After validate_semantic_high() returns, this updates the registry\n    with the debate outcome and adjusts severity if downgraded.\n    \"\"\"\n    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '')\n    if not stable_id:\n        logger.warning(\"Finding has no stable_id; cannot record verdict\")\n        return\n\n    registry.record_debate_verdict(stable_id, verdict, transcript_path)\n\n    if verdict.startswith(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\"):\n        new_severity = verdict.replace(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\", \"\")\n        logger.info(\n            \"Finding %s downgraded from HIGH to %s by debate verdict\",\n            stable_id[:8], new_severity,\n        )\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":194,"oldLines":7,"newStart":194,"newLines":22,"lines":["             f\"Template overhead ({template_bytes}B) exceeds budget ({template_budget}B)\"","         )"," ","-    # 30KB hard limit\n\n# Proportional allocation (BF-7 resolution)\nBUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT = 0.60     # 60% = 18,432 bytes\nBUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT = 0.20   # 20% = 6,144 bytes\nBUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT = 0.15    # 15% = 4,608 bytes\nBUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT = 0.05         # 5%  = 1,536 bytes\n\nTRUNCATION_MARKER = \"[TRUNCATED: {} bytes omitted from '{}']\"\n\n\n# --- Debate Protocol Constants (BF-6 resolution) ---\n\nDEBATE_TOKEN_CAP = 5_000  # max tokens per finding debate\n\n# Rubric weights for deterministic judge\nRUBRIC_WEIGHTS = {\n    \"evidence_quality\": 0.30,\n    \"impact_specificity\": 0.25,\n    \"logical_coherence\": 0.25,\n    \"concession_handling\": 0.20,\n}\n\nVERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD = 0.15  # margin > 0.15 = clear winner\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Scores for one side of the debate (prosecutor or defender).\"\"\"\n    evidence_quality: int = 0      # 0-3\n    impact_specificity: int = 0    # 0-3\n    logical_coherence: int = 0     # 0-3\n    concession_handling: int = 0   # 0-3\n\n    @property\n    def weighted_score(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Compute weighted score normalized to [0.0, 1.0].\"\"\"\n        raw = (\n            self.evidence_quality * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"evidence_quality\"]\n            + self.impact_specificity * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"impact_specificity\"]\n            + self.logical_coherence * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"logical_coherence\"]\n            + self.concession_handling * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"concession_handling\"]\n        )\n        return raw / 3.0  # Normalize: max raw per criterion is 3\n\n\n# --- Prompt Templates ---\n\nPROSECUTOR_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the PROSECUTOR in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding IS correctly classified as HIGH severity.\nProvide concrete evidence from the spec and roadmap.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: HIGH\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\nDEFENDER_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the DEFENDER in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding should be DOWNGRADED from HIGH.\nIf downgrading, recommend MEDIUM or LOW with justification.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: <MEDIUM or LOW>\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\n\n# --- Semantic Check Request ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass SemanticCheckRequest:\n    \"\"\"Input to the semantic LLM layer for one dimension.\"\"\"\n    dimension: str\n    spec_sections: list[Any]       # SpecSection objects\n    roadmap_sections: list[Any]    # SpecSection objects\n    structural_findings: list[Finding]\n    prior_findings_summary: str\n\n\n# --- Core Functions ---\n\ndef _truncate_to_budget(content: str, budget_bytes: int, heading: str = \"content\") -> str:\n    \"\"\"Tail-truncate content to fit within byte budget on line boundaries.\n\n    BF-7 resolution: proportional budget allocation with tail-truncation.\n    FR-4.2: truncation markers include section heading.\n    \"\"\"\n    encoded = content.encode(\"utf-8\")\n    if len(encoded) <= budget_bytes:\n        return content\n\n    omitted = len(encoded) - budget_bytes\n    marker = TRUNCATION_MARKER.format(omitted, heading)\n    marker_bytes = len(marker.encode(\"utf-8\")) + 1  # +1 for newline\n    target = budget_bytes - marker_bytes\n\n    if target <= 0:\n        return marker\n\n    # Truncate on line boundary\n    truncated = encoded[:target].decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\")\n    last_newline = truncated.rfind(\"\\n\")\n    if last_newline > 0:\n        truncated = truncated[:last_newline]\n\n    return truncated + \"\\n\" + marker\n\n\ndef build_semantic_prompt(request: SemanticCheckRequest) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build a chunked prompt for one semantic check.\n\n    Enforces NFR-3: prompt size <= 30KB via proportional budget\n    allocation (BF-7 resolution).\n    \"\"\"\n    template_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT)\n    spec_roadmap_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT)\n    structural_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT)\n    prior_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT)\n\n    # Build template\n    template = (\n        f\"## Semantic Fidelity Check: {request.dimension}\\n\\n\"\n        \"Analyze the following spec and roadmap sections for semantic \"\n        \"deviations NOT already covered by structural checkers.\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Already Checked (do NOT re-report)\\n\"\n    )\n\n    template_bytes = len(template.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    if template_bytes > template_budget:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"Template overhead ({template_bytes}B) exceeds budget ({template_budget}B)\"\n        )\n\n    # Truncation priority (FR-4.2): prior summary first, then structural, then spec/roadmap\n    # Truncate prior findings summary (least critical — cut first)\n    prior_text = _truncate_to_budget(\n        request.prior_findings_summary, prior_budget, heading=\"Prior Findings Summary\",\n    )\n\n    # Truncate structural findings context (secondary)\n    structural_text = \"\\n\".join(\n        f\"- [{f.severity}] {f.dimension}: {f.description}\"\n        for f in request.structural_findings\n    )\n    structural_text = _truncate_to_budget(\n        structural_text, structural_budget, heading=\"Structural Findings Context\",\n    )\n\n    # Truncate spec+roadmap sections (last resort)\n    spec_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.spec_sections\n    )\n    roadmap_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.roadmap_sections\n    )\n    combined = f\"### Spec Sections\\n{spec_text}\\n\\n### Roadmap Sections\\n{roadmap_text}\"\n    combined = _truncate_to_budget(\n        combined, spec_roadmap_budget, heading=\"Spec/Roadmap Sections\",\n    )\n\n    prompt = (\n        f\"{template}\"\n        f\"{structural_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"### Prior Findings (for anchoring)\\n{prior_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"{combined}\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Instructions\\n\"\n        \"Report only NEW semantic deviations. Assign severity: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.\\n\"\n        \"For each finding, provide: dimension, description, severity, evidence.\\n\"\n    )\n\n    # Final enforcement assert\n    prompt_size = len(prompt.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    assert prompt_size <= MAX_PROMPT_BYTES, (\n        f\"Prompt size {prompt_size}B exceeds {MAX_PROMPT_BYTES}B budget\"\n    )\n\n    return prompt\n\n\ndef score_argument(response_yaml: dict) -> RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Score a debate argument using the deterministic rubric.\n\n    BF-6 resolution: automated judge with 4-criterion rubric.\n    Scores 0-3 per criterion based on evidence in the response.\n    \"\"\"\n    evidence_points = response_yaml.get(\"evidence_points\", [])\n    argument = response_yaml.get(\"argument\", \"\")\n    confidence = response_yaml.get(\"confidence\", 0.0)\n\n    # Evidence quality: count of concrete evidence points\n    eq = min(len(evidence_points), 3)\n\n    # Impact specificity: argument length and specificity\n    word_count = len(argument.split())\n    if word_count >= 100:\n        imp = 3\n    elif word_count >= 50:\n        imp = 2\n    elif word_count >= 20:\n        imp = 1\n    else:\n        imp = 0\n\n    # Logical coherence: confidence level maps to coherence\n    if confidence >= 0.8:\n        lc = 3\n    elif confidence >= 0.6:\n        lc = 2\n    elif confidence >= 0.4:\n        lc = 1\n    else:\n        lc = 0\n\n    # Concession handling: presence of caveats/qualifications\n    concession_markers = [\"however\", \"although\", \"while\", \"despite\", \"caveat\", \"limitation\"]\n    concessions = sum(1 for m in concession_markers if m in argument.lower())\n    ch = min(concessions, 3)\n\n    return RubricScores(\n        evidence_quality=eq,\n        impact_specificity=imp,\n        logical_coherence=lc,\n        concession_handling=ch,\n    )\n\n\ndef judge_verdict(\n    prosecutor_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_recommendation: str = \"MEDIUM\",\n) -> tuple[str, float]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic judge: compare weighted scores, apply margin threshold.\n\n    BF-6 resolution:\n    - Prosecutor margin > 0.15 -> CONFIRM_HIGH\n    - Defender margin > 0.15 -> DOWNGRADE_TO_{recommendation}\n    - Margin <= 0.15 (tiebreak) -> CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative)\n\n    Returns (verdict, margin).\n    \"\"\"\n    p_score = prosecutor_scores.weighted_score\n    d_score = defender_scores.weighted_score\n    margin = p_score - d_score\n\n    if margin > VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n    elif margin < -VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return f\"DOWNGRADE_TO_{defender_recommendation}\", margin\n    else:\n        # Tiebreak: conservative -- uncertain cases stay HIGH\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n\n\n# --- Debate-Registry Wiring (Task 4.3) ---\n\ndef wire_debate_verdict(\n    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry from convergence module\n    finding: Finding,\n    verdict: str,\n    transcript_path: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Wire debate verdict into the deviation registry.\n\n    After validate_semantic_high() returns, this updates the registry\n    with the debate outcome and adjusts severity if downgraded.\n    \"\"\"\n    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '')\n    if not stable_id:\n        logger.warning(\"Finding has no stable_id; cannot record verdict\")\n        return\n\n    registry.record_debate_verdict(stable_id, verdict, transcript_path)\n\n    if verdict.startswith(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\"):\n        new_severity = verdict.replace(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\", \"\")\n        logger.info(\n            \"Finding %s downgraded from HIGH to %s by debate verdict\",\n            stable_id[:8], new_severity,\n        )\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":5,"oldLines":11,"newStart":5,"newLines":15,"lines":[" \"\"\""," from __future__ import annotations"," ","+import json"," import logging","+from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor"," from dataclasses import dataclass, field"," from pathlib import Path","-from typing import Any","+from typing import Any, Callable"," ","+import yaml","+"," from .models import Finding"," "," logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Are descriptions adequate and unambiguous?\n    \"contradiction_detection\", # Do spec sections contradict each other?\n    \"completeness_coverage\",   # Are all spec aspects addressed in roadmap?\n    \"architectural_alignment\", # Does the roadmap's design match spec intent?\n})\n\n\n# --- T04.01: run_semantic_layer() Entry Point ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass SemanticLayerResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of running the semantic layer.\"\"\"\n    findings: list[Finding]\n    prompts_sent: int = 0\n    debates_triggered: int = 0\n    debate_verdicts: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n\ndef run_semantic_layer(\n    spec_sections: list[Any],\n    roadmap_sections: list[Any],\n    structural_findings: list[Finding],\n    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry\n    output_dir: Path,\n    *,\n    claude_process_factory: Callable | None = None,\n) -> SemanticLayerResult:\n    \"\"\"Entry point for the residual semantic layer (FR-4).\n\n    Receives only dimensions/aspects NOT covered by structural checkers.\n    Uses per-section chunked input. Includes structural findings as context.\n    Tags all findings with source_layer=\"semantic\".\n    Prior findings from registry memory included in prompt.\n\n    Args:\n        spec_sections: Parsed SpecSection objects from the spec.\n        roadmap_sections: Parsed SpecSection objects from the roadmap.\n        structural_findings: Findings from structural checkers (context, not re-checked).\n        registry: Active DeviationRegistry for prior findings and verdict recording.\n        output_dir: Directory for debate artifacts.\n        claude_process_factory: Optional factory for LLM calls (enables testing).\n\n    Returns:\n        SemanticLayerResult with findings and debate metadata.\n    \"\"\"\n    prior_summary = registry.get_prior_findings_summary(max_entries=50)\n    result = SemanticLayerResult(findings=[])\n\n    for dimension in sorted(SEMANTIC_DIMENSIONS):\n        request = SemanticCheckRequest(\n            dimension=dimension,\n            spec_sections=spec_sections,\n            roadmap_sections=roadmap_sections,\n            structural_findings=structural_findings,\n            prior_findings_summary=prior_summary,\n        )\n\n        prompt = build_semantic_prompt(request)\n        result.prompts_sent += 1\n\n        # Execute semantic check via LLM\n        if claude_process_factory is not None:\n            findings = _execute_semantic_check(\n                prompt, dimension, claude_process_factory,\n            )\n        else:\n            # Without a factory, return empty (no live LLM available)\n            logger.warning(\n                \"No claude_process_factory provided; skipping LLM call for %s\",\n                dimension,\n            )\n            findings = []\n\n        # Tag all findings with source_layer=\"semantic\"\n        for f in findings:\n            if hasattr(f, 'source_layer'):\n                object.__setattr__(f, 'source_layer', 'semantic') if hasattr(type(f), '__dataclass_fields__') else setattr(f, 'source_layer', 'semantic')\n\n        # Trigger debate for HIGH findings (FR-4.1)\n        for f in findings:\n            if f.severity == \"HIGH\":\n                result.debates_triggered += 1\n                verdict = validate_semantic_high(\n                    finding=f,\n                    registry=registry,\n                    output_dir=output_dir,\n                    claude_process_factory=claude_process_factory,\n                )\n                result.debate_verdicts[f.stable_id] = verdict\n\n        result.findings.extend(findings)\n\n    return result\n\n\ndef _execute_semantic_check(\n    prompt: str,\n    dimension: str,\n    claude_process_factory: Callable,\n) -> list[Finding]:\n    \"\"\"Execute a single semantic check via LLM and parse response into Findings.\n\n    The factory should return an object with a run(prompt) method that\n    returns the LLM response string. Response is parsed as YAML.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .convergence import compute_stable_id\n\n    try:\n        process = claude_process_factory()\n        response_text = process.run(prompt)\n    except Exception as exc:\n        logger.error(\"Semantic check failed for %s: %s\", dimension, exc)\n        return []\n\n    # Parse YAML response\n    try:\n        parsed = yaml.safe_load(response_text)\n    except (yaml.YAMLError, AttributeError):\n        logger.warning(\"Failed to parse semantic response YAML for %s\", dimension)\n        return []\n\n    if not isinstance(parsed, dict):\n        return []\n\n    findings_data = parsed.get(\"findings\", [])\n    if not isinstance(findings_data, list):\n        return []\n\n    findings: list[Finding] = []\n    for entry in findings_data:\n        if not isinstance(entry, dict):\n            continue\n        description = entry.get(\"description\", \"\")\n        severity = entry.get(\"severity\", \"MEDIUM\")\n        location = entry.get(\"location\", f\"semantic:{dimension}\")\n        rule_id = f\"semantic_{dimension}\"\n        stable_id = compute_stable_id(dimension, rule_id, location, rule_id)\n\n        findings.append(Finding(\n            id=f\"{dimension}-semantic-{stable_id[:8]}\",\n            severity=severity,\n            dimension=dimension,\n            description=description,\n            location=location,\n            evidence=entry.get(\"evidence\", \"\"),\n            fix_guidance=entry.get(\"fix_guidance\", f\"Review {dimension} for semantic issues\"),\n            status=\"ACTIVE\",\n            source_layer=\"semantic\",\n            rule_id=rule_id,\n            spec_quote=entry.get(\"spec_quote\", \"\"),\n            roadmap_quote=entry.get(\"roadmap_quote\", \"\"),\n            stable_id=stable_id,\n        ))\n\n    return findings\n\n\n# --- T04.03: validate_semantic_high() Debate Protocol ---\n\ndef validate_semantic_high(\n    finding: Finding,\n    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry\n    output_dir: Path,\n    *,\n    claude_process_factory: Callable | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Orchestrate lightweight adversarial debate for a semantic HIGH finding.\n\n    Implements FR-4.1 protocol steps 1-7:\n    1. Build prosecutor + defender prompts from finding context\n    2. Execute both via ClaudeProcess in parallel (2 LLM calls)\n    3. Parse YAML responses; default scores to 0 on parse failure\n    4. Score both via score_argument() against 4-criterion rubric\n    5. Compute verdict via judge_verdict()\n    6. Write debate YAML to output_dir/debates/{stable_id}/debate.yaml\n    7. Call wire_debate_verdict() to update registry\n\n    Args:\n        finding: The semantic HIGH finding to validate.\n        registry: Active deviation registry for verdict recording.\n        output_dir: Directory for debate output artifacts.\n        claude_process_factory: Optional factory for ClaudeProcess creation.\n\n    Returns:\n        Verdict string: \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", \"DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM\", or \"DOWNGRADE_TO_LOW\".\n    \"\"\"\n    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '') or ''\n\n    # Step 1: Build prompts\n    prosecutor_prompt = PROSECUTOR_TEMPLATE.format(\n        dimension=finding.dimension,\n        rule_id=getattr(finding, 'rule_id', ''),\n        description=finding.description,\n        spec_quote=getattr(finding, 'spec_quote', ''),\n        roadmap_quote=getattr(finding, 'roadmap_quote', ''),\n        spec_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",\n        roadmap_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",\n    )\n    defender_prompt = DEFENDER_TEMPLATE.format(\n        dimension=finding.dimension,\n        rule_id=getattr(finding, 'rule_id', ''),\n        description=finding.description,\n        spec_quote=getattr(finding, 'spec_quote', ''),\n        roadmap_quote=getattr(finding, 'roadmap_quote', ''),\n        spec_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",\n        roadmap_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",\n    )\n\n    # Step 2: Execute both in parallel\n    prosecutor_yaml: dict = {}\n    defender_yaml: dict = {}\n\n    if claude_process_factory is not None:\n        def _run_side(prompt: str) -> dict:\n            try:\n                process = claude_process_factory()\n                response = process.run(prompt)\n                parsed = yaml.safe_load(response)\n                return parsed if isinstance(parsed, dict) else {}\n            except Exception:\n                return {}\n\n        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2) as pool:\n            p_future = pool.submit(_run_side, prosecutor_prompt)\n            d_future = pool.submit(_run_side, defender_prompt)\n            prosecutor_yaml = p_future.result()\n            defender_yaml = d_future.result()\n\n    # Step 3 & 4: Score both sides (defaults to 0 on parse failure)\n    prosecutor_scores = score_argument(prosecutor_yaml)\n    defender_scores = score_argument(defender_yaml)\n\n    # Extract defender recommendation\n    defender_rec = defender_yaml.get(\"recommended_severity\", \"MEDIUM\")\n    if defender_rec not in (\"MEDIUM\", \"LOW\"):\n        defender_rec = \"MEDIUM\"\n\n    # DeviationRegistry\n    output_dir: Path,\n    *,\n    claude_process_factory: Callable | None = None,\n) -> SemanticLayerResult:\n    \"\"\"Entry point for the residual semantic layer (FR-4).\n\n    Receives only dimensions/aspects NOT covered by structural checkers.\n    Uses per-section chunked input. skipping LLM call for %s\",\n                dimension,\n            )\n            findings = []\n\n        # Tag all findings with source_layer=\"semantic\"\n        for f in findings:\n            if hasattr(f, 'source_layer'):\n                object.__setattr__(f, 'source_layer', 'semantic') if hasattr(type(f), '__dataclass_fields__') else setattr(f, 'source_layer', 'semantic')\n\n        # Trigger debate for HIGH findings (FR-4.1)\n        for f in findings:\n            if f.severity == \"HIGH\":\n                result.debates_triggered += 1\n                verdict = validate_semantic_high(\n                    finding=f,\n                    registry=registry,\n                    output_dir=output_dir,\n                    claude_process_factory=claude_process_factory,\n                )\n                result.debate_verdicts[f.stable_id] = verdict\n\n        result.findings.extend(findings)\n\n    return result\n\n\ndef _execute_semantic_check(\n    prompt: str,\n    dimension: str,\n    claude_process_factory: Callable,\n) -> list[Finding]:\n    \"\"\"Execute a single semantic check via LLM and parse response into Findings.\n\n    The factory should return an object with a run(prompt) method that\n    returns the LLM response string. Response is parsed as YAML.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .convergence import compute_stable_id\n\n    try:\n        process = claude_process_factory()\n        response_text = process.run(prompt)\n    except Exception as exc:\n        logger.error(\"Semantic check failed for %s: %s\", dimension, exc)\n        return []\n\n    # Parse YAML response\n    try:\n        parsed = yaml.safe_load(response_text)\n    except (yaml.YAMLError, AttributeError):\n        logger.warning(\"Failed to parse semantic response YAML for %s\", dimension)\n        return []\n\n    if not isinstance(parsed, dict):\n        return []\n\n    findings_data = parsed.get(\"findings\", [])\n    if not isinstance(findings_data, list):\n        return []\n\n    findings: list[Finding] = []\n    for entry in findings_data:\n        if not isinstance(entry, dict):\n            continue\n        description = entry.get(\"description\", \"\")\n        severity = entry.get(\"severity\", \"MEDIUM\")\n        location = entry.get(\"location\", f\"semantic:{dimension}\")\n        rule_id = f\"semantic_{dimension}\"\n        stable_id = compute_stable_id(dimension, rule_id, location, rule_id)\n\n        findings.append(Finding(\n            id=f\"{dimension}-semantic-{stable_id[:8]}\",\n            severity=severity,\n            dimension=dimension,\n            description=description,\n            location=location,\n            evidence=entry.get(\"evidence\", \"\"),\n            fix_guidance=entry.get(\"fix_guidance\", f\"Review {dimension} for semantic issues\"),\n            status=\"ACTIVE\",\n            source_layer=\"semantic\",\n            rule_id=rule_id,\n            spec_quote=entry.get(\"spec_quote\", \"\"),\n            roadmap_quote=entry.get(\"roadmap_quote\", \"\"),\n            stable_id=stable_id,\n        ))\n\n    return findings\n\n\n# --- T04.03: validate_semantic_high() Debate Protocol ---\n\ndef validate_semantic_high(\n    finding: Finding,\n    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry\n    output_dir: Path,\n    *,\n    claude_process_factory: Callable | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Orchestrate lightweight adversarial debate for a semantic HIGH finding.\n\n    Implements FR-4.1 protocol steps 1-7:\n    1. Build prosecutor + defender prompts from finding context\n    2. Execute both via ClaudeProcess in parallel (2 LLM calls)\n    3. Parse YAML responses; default scores to 0 on parse failure\n    4. Score both via score_argument() against 4-criterion rubric\n    5. Compute verdict via judge_verdict()\n    6. Write debate YAML to output_dir/debates/{stable_id}/debate.yaml\n    7. Call wire_debate_verdict() to update registry\n\n    Args:\n        finding: The semantic HIGH finding to validate.\n        registry: Active deviation registry for verdict recording.\n        output_dir: Directory for debate output artifacts.\n        claude_process_factory: Optional factory for ClaudeProcess creation.\n\n    Returns:\n        Verdict string: \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", \"DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM\", or \"DOWNGRADE_TO_LOW\".\n    \"\"\"\n    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '') or ''\n\n    # Step 1: Build prompts\n    prosecutor_prompt = PROSECUTOR_TEMPLATE.format(\n        dimension=finding.dimension,\n        rule_id=getattr(finding, 'rule_id', ''),\n        description=finding.description,\n        spec_quote=getattr(finding, 'spec_quote', ''),\n        roadmap_quote=getattr(finding, 'roadmap_quote', ''),\n        spec_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",\n        roadmap_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",\n    )\n    defender_prompt = DEFENDER_TEMPLATE.format(\n        dimension=finding.dimension,\n        rule_id=getattr(finding, 'rule_id', ''),\n        description=finding.description,\n        spec_quote=getattr(finding, 'spec_quote', ''),\n        roadmap_quote=getattr(finding, 'roadmap_quote', ''),\n        spec_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",\n        roadmap_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",\n    )\n\n    # Step 2: Execute both in parallel\n    prosecutor_yaml: dict = {}\n    defender_yaml: dict = {}\n\n    if claude_process_factory is not None:\n        def _run_side(prompt: str) -> dict:\n            try:\n                process = claude_process_factory()\n                response = process.run(prompt)\n                parsed = yaml.safe_load(response)\n                return parsed if isinstance(parsed, dict) else {}\n            except Exception:\n                return {}\n\n        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2) as pool:\n            p_future = pool.submit(_run_side, prosecutor_prompt)\n            d_future = pool.submit(_run_side, defender_prompt)\n            prosecutor_yaml = p_future.result()\n            defender_yaml = d_future.result()\n\n    # Step 3 & 4: Score both sides (defaults to 0 on parse failure)\n    prosecutor_scores = score_argument(prosecutor_yaml)\n    defender_scores = score_argument(defender_yaml)\n\n    # Extract defender recommendation\n    defender_rec = defender_yaml.get(\"recommended_severity\", \"MEDIUM\")\n    if defender_rec not in (\"MEDIUM\", \"LOW\"):\n        defender_rec = \"MEDIUM\"\n\n    # Step 7: Wire verdict into registry (T04.04)\n    wire_debate_verdict(registry, finding, verdict, str(debate_yaml_path))\n\n    logger.info(\n        \"Debate for %s: verdict=%s margin=%.3f\",\n        stable_id[:8], verdict, margin,\n    )\n\n    return verdict","originalFile":"\"\"\"Residual semantic layer with adversarial validation.\n\nImplements FR-4 (semantic checking), FR-5 (chunked comparison),\nand the lightweight debate protocol (BF-6 resolution).\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport logging\nfrom concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Callable\n\nimport yaml\n\nfrom .models import Finding\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# --- Prompt Budget Constants (BF-7 / NFR-3) ---\n\nMAX_PROMPT_BYTES = 30_720  # 30KB hard limit\n\n# Proportional allocation (BF-7 resolution)\nBUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT = 0.60     # 60% = 18,432 bytes\nBUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT = 0.20   # 20% = 6,144 bytes\nBUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT = 0.15    # 15% = 4,608 bytes\nBUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT = 0.05         # 5%  = 1,536 bytes\n\nTRUNCATION_MARKER = \"[TRUNCATED: {} bytes omitted from '{}']\"\n\n\n# --- Debate Protocol Constants (BF-6 resolution) ---\n\nDEBATE_TOKEN_CAP = 5_000  # max tokens per finding debate\n\n# Rubric weights for deterministic judge\nRUBRIC_WEIGHTS = {\n    \"evidence_quality\": 0.30,\n    \"impact_specificity\": 0.25,\n    \"logical_coherence\": 0.25,\n    \"concession_handling\": 0.20,\n}\n\nVERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD = 0.15  # margin > 0.15 = clear winner\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Scores for one side of the debate (prosecutor or defender).\"\"\"\n    evidence_quality: int = 0      # 0-3\n    impact_specificity: int = 0    # 0-3\n    logical_coherence: int = 0     # 0-3\n    concession_handling: int = 0   # 0-3\n\n    @property\n    def weighted_score(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Compute weighted score normalized to [0.0, 1.0].\"\"\"\n        raw = (\n            self.evidence_quality * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"evidence_quality\"]\n            + self.impact_specificity * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"impact_specificity\"]\n            + self.logical_coherence * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"logical_coherence\"]\n            + self.concession_handling * RUBRIC_WEIGHTS[\"concession_handling\"]\n        )\n        return raw / 3.0  # Normalize: max raw per criterion is 3\n\n\n# --- Prompt Templates ---\n\nPROSECUTOR_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the PROSECUTOR in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding IS correctly classified as HIGH severity.\nProvide concrete evidence from the spec and roadmap.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: HIGH\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\nDEFENDER_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"You are the DEFENDER in a severity validation debate.\n\n## Finding Under Review\n- **Dimension**: {dimension}\n- **Rule ID**: {rule_id}\n- **Current Severity**: HIGH\n- **Description**: {description}\n- **Spec Evidence**: {spec_quote}\n- **Roadmap Evidence**: {roadmap_quote}\n\n## Spec Context\n{spec_context}\n\n## Roadmap Context\n{roadmap_context}\n\n## Your Task\nArgue that this finding should be DOWNGRADED from HIGH.\nIf downgrading, recommend MEDIUM or LOW with justification.\n\n## Response Format (YAML)\n```yaml\nargument: |\n  <your argument>\nevidence_points:\n  - <point 1>\n  - <point 2>\nrecommended_severity: <MEDIUM or LOW>\nconfidence: <0.0-1.0>\n```\"\"\"\n\n\n# --- Semantic Check Request ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass SemanticCheckRequest:\n    \"\"\"Input to the semantic LLM layer for one dimension.\"\"\"\n    dimension: str\n    spec_sections: list[Any]       # SpecSection objects\n    roadmap_sections: list[Any]    # SpecSection objects\n    structural_findings: list[Finding]\n    prior_findings_summary: str\n\n\n# --- Core Functions ---\n\ndef _truncate_to_budget(content: str, budget_bytes: int, heading: str = \"content\") -> str:\n    \"\"\"Tail-truncate content to fit within byte budget on line boundaries.\n\n    BF-7 resolution: proportional budget allocation with tail-truncation.\n    FR-4.2: truncation markers include section heading.\n    \"\"\"\n    encoded = content.encode(\"utf-8\")\n    if len(encoded) <= budget_bytes:\n        return content\n\n    omitted = len(encoded) - budget_bytes\n    marker = TRUNCATION_MARKER.format(omitted, heading)\n    marker_bytes = len(marker.encode(\"utf-8\")) + 1  # +1 for newline\n    target = budget_bytes - marker_bytes\n\n    if target <= 0:\n        return marker\n\n    # Truncate on line boundary\n    truncated = encoded[:target].decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\")\n    last_newline = truncated.rfind(\"\\n\")\n    if last_newline > 0:\n        truncated = truncated[:last_newline]\n\n    return truncated + \"\\n\" + marker\n\n\ndef build_semantic_prompt(request: SemanticCheckRequest) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build a chunked prompt for one semantic check.\n\n    Enforces NFR-3: prompt size <= 30KB via proportional budget\n    allocation (BF-7 resolution).\n    \"\"\"\n    template_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_TEMPLATE_PCT)\n    spec_roadmap_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_SPEC_ROADMAP_PCT)\n    structural_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_STRUCTURAL_CTX_PCT)\n    prior_budget = int(MAX_PROMPT_BYTES * BUDGET_PRIOR_SUMMARY_PCT)\n\n    # Build template\n    template = (\n        f\"## Semantic Fidelity Check: {request.dimension}\\n\\n\"\n        \"Analyze the following spec and roadmap sections for semantic \"\n        \"deviations NOT already covered by structural checkers.\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Already Checked (do NOT re-report)\\n\"\n    )\n\n    template_bytes = len(template.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    if template_bytes > template_budget:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"Template overhead ({template_bytes}B) exceeds budget ({template_budget}B)\"\n        )\n\n    # Truncation priority (FR-4.2): prior summary first, then structural, then spec/roadmap\n    # Truncate prior findings summary (least critical — cut first)\n    prior_text = _truncate_to_budget(\n        request.prior_findings_summary, prior_budget, heading=\"Prior Findings Summary\",\n    )\n\n    # Truncate structural findings context (secondary)\n    structural_text = \"\\n\".join(\n        f\"- [{f.severity}] {f.dimension}: {f.description}\"\n        for f in request.structural_findings\n    )\n    structural_text = _truncate_to_budget(\n        structural_text, structural_budget, heading=\"Structural Findings Context\",\n    )\n\n    # Truncate spec+roadmap sections (last resort)\n    spec_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.spec_sections\n    )\n    roadmap_text = \"\\n\\n\".join(\n        f\"#### {s.heading}\\n{s.content}\" if hasattr(s, 'heading') else str(s)\n        for s in request.roadmap_sections\n    )\n    combined = f\"### Spec Sections\\n{spec_text}\\n\\n### Roadmap Sections\\n{roadmap_text}\"\n    combined = _truncate_to_budget(\n        combined, spec_roadmap_budget, heading=\"Spec/Roadmap Sections\",\n    )\n\n    prompt = (\n        f\"{template}\"\n        f\"{structural_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"### Prior Findings (for anchoring)\\n{prior_text}\\n\\n\"\n        f\"{combined}\\n\\n\"\n        \"### Instructions\\n\"\n        \"Report only NEW semantic deviations. Assign severity: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.\\n\"\n        \"For each finding, provide: dimension, description, severity, evidence.\\n\"\n    )\n\n    # Final enforcement assert\n    prompt_size = len(prompt.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    assert prompt_size <= MAX_PROMPT_BYTES, (\n        f\"Prompt size {prompt_size}B exceeds {MAX_PROMPT_BYTES}B budget\"\n    )\n\n    return prompt\n\n\ndef score_argument(response_yaml: dict) -> RubricScores:\n    \"\"\"Score a debate argument using the deterministic rubric.\n\n    BF-6 resolution: automated judge with 4-criterion rubric.\n    Scores 0-3 per criterion based on evidence in the response.\n    \"\"\"\n    evidence_points = response_yaml.get(\"evidence_points\", [])\n    argument = response_yaml.get(\"argument\", \"\")\n    confidence = response_yaml.get(\"confidence\", 0.0)\n\n    # Evidence quality: count of concrete evidence points\n    eq = min(len(evidence_points), 3)\n\n    # Impact specificity: argument length and specificity\n    word_count = len(argument.split())\n    if word_count >= 100:\n        imp = 3\n    elif word_count >= 50:\n        imp = 2\n    elif word_count >= 20:\n        imp = 1\n    else:\n        imp = 0\n\n    # Logical coherence: confidence level maps to coherence\n    if confidence >= 0.8:\n        lc = 3\n    elif confidence >= 0.6:\n        lc = 2\n    elif confidence >= 0.4:\n        lc = 1\n    else:\n        lc = 0\n\n    # Concession handling: presence of caveats/qualifications\n    concession_markers = [\"however\", \"although\", \"while\", \"despite\", \"caveat\", \"limitation\"]\n    concessions = sum(1 for m in concession_markers if m in argument.lower())\n    ch = min(concessions, 3)\n\n    return RubricScores(\n        evidence_quality=eq,\n        impact_specificity=imp,\n        logical_coherence=lc,\n        concession_handling=ch,\n    )\n\n\ndef judge_verdict(\n    prosecutor_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_scores: RubricScores,\n    defender_recommendation: str = \"MEDIUM\",\n) -> tuple[str, float]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic judge: compare weighted scores, apply margin threshold.\n\n    BF-6 resolution:\n    - Prosecutor margin > 0.15 -> CONFIRM_HIGH\n    - Defender margin > 0.15 -> DOWNGRADE_TO_{recommendation}\n    - Margin <= 0.15 (tiebreak) -> CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative)\n\n    Returns (verdict, margin).\n    \"\"\"\n    p_score = prosecutor_scores.weighted_score\n    d_score = defender_scores.weighted_score\n    margin = p_score - d_score\n\n    if margin > VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n    elif margin < -VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD:\n        return f\"DOWNGRADE_TO_{defender_recommendation}\", margin\n    else:\n        # Tiebreak: conservative -- uncertain cases stay HIGH\n        return \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", margin\n\n\n# --- Debate-Registry Wiring (Task 4.3) ---\n\ndef wire_debate_verdict(\n    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry from convergence module\n    finding: Finding,\n    verdict: str,\n    transcript_path: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Wire debate verdict into the deviation registry.\n\n    After validate_semantic_high() returns, this updates the registry\n    with the debate outcome and adjusts severity if downgraded.\n    \"\"\"\n    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '')\n    if not stable_id:\n        logger.warning(\"Finding has no stable_id; cannot record verdict\")\n        return\n\n    registry.record_debate_verdict(stable_id, verdict, transcript_path)\n\n    if verdict.startswith(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\"):\n        new_severity = verdict.replace(\"DOWNGRADE_TO_\", \"\")\n        logger.info(\n            \"Finding %s downgraded from HIGH to %s by debate verdict\",\n            stable_id[:8], new_severity,\n        )\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":346,"oldLines":3,"newStart":346,"newLines":292,"lines":["             \"Finding %s downgraded from HIGH to %s by debate verdict\",","             stable_id[:8], new_severity,","         )","+","+","+# --- Structural dimension set (for filtering) ---","+","+# Dimensions covered by structural checkers (from structural_checkers.CHECKER_REGISTRY keys).","+# Semantic layer processes only aspects NOT in this set.","+STRUCTURAL_DIMENSIONS = frozenset({\"signatures\", \"data_models\", \"gates\", \"cli\", \"nfrs\"})","+","+# Semantic-only dimensions: judgment-dependent checks not covered by structural rules","+SEMANTIC_DIMENSIONS = frozenset({","+    \"prose_sufficiency\",       # Are descriptions adequate and unambiguous?","+    \"contradiction_detection\", # Do spec sections contradict each other?","+    \"completeness_coverage\",   # Are all spec aspects addressed in roadmap?","+    \"architectural_alignment\", # Does the roadmap's design match spec intent?","+})","+","+","+# --- T04.01: run_semantic_layer() Entry Point ---","+","+@dataclass","+class SemanticLayerResult:","+    \"\"\"Result of running the semantic layer.\"\"\"","+    findings: list[Finding]","+    prompts_sent: int = 0","+    debates_triggered: int = 0","+    debate_verdicts: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)","+","+","+def run_semantic_layer(","+    spec_sections: list[Any],","+    roadmap_sections: list[Any],","+    structural_findings: list[Finding],","+    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry","+    output_dir: Path,","+    *,","+    claude_process_factory: Callable | None = None,","+) -> SemanticLayerResult:","+    \"\"\"Entry point for the residual semantic layer (FR-4).","+","+    Receives only dimensions/aspects NOT covered by structural checkers.","+    Uses per-section chunked input. Includes structural findings as context.","+    Tags all findings with source_layer=\"semantic\".","+    Prior findings from registry memory included in prompt.","+","+    Args:","+        spec_sections: Parsed SpecSection objects from the spec.","+        roadmap_sections: Parsed SpecSection objects from the roadmap.","+        structural_findings: Findings from structural checkers (context, not re-checked).","+        registry: Active DeviationRegistry for prior findings and verdict recording.","+        output_dir: Directory for debate artifacts.","+        claude_process_factory: Optional factory for LLM calls (enables testing).","+","+    Returns:","+        SemanticLayerResult with findings and debate metadata.","+    \"\"\"","+    prior_summary = registry.get_prior_findings_summary(max_entries=50)","+    result = SemanticLayerResult(findings=[])","+","+    for dimension in sorted(SEMANTIC_DIMENSIONS):","+        request = SemanticCheckRequest(","+            dimension=dimension,","+            spec_sections=spec_sections,","+            roadmap_sections=roadmap_sections,","+            structural_findings=structural_findings,","+            prior_findings_summary=prior_summary,","+        )","+","+        prompt = build_semantic_prompt(request)","+        result.prompts_sent += 1","+","+        # Execute semantic check via LLM","+        if claude_process_factory is not None:","+            findings = _execute_semantic_check(","+                prompt, dimension, claude_process_factory,","+            )","+        else:","+            # Without a factory, return empty (no live LLM available)","+            logger.warning(","+                \"No claude_process_factory provided; skipping LLM call for %s\",","+                dimension,","+            )","+            findings = []","+","+        # Tag all findings with source_layer=\"semantic\"","+        for f in findings:","+            if hasattr(f, 'source_layer'):","+                object.__setattr__(f, 'source_layer', 'semantic') if hasattr(type(f), '__dataclass_fields__') else setattr(f, 'source_layer', 'semantic')","+","+        # Trigger debate for HIGH findings (FR-4.1)","+        for f in findings:","+            if f.severity == \"HIGH\":","+                result.debates_triggered += 1","+                verdict = validate_semantic_high(","+                    finding=f,","+                    registry=registry,","+                    output_dir=output_dir,","+                    claude_process_factory=claude_process_factory,","+                )","+                result.debate_verdicts[f.stable_id] = verdict","+","+        result.findings.extend(findings)","+","+    return result","+","+","+def _execute_semantic_check(","+    prompt: str,","+    dimension: str,","+    claude_process_factory: Callable,","+) -> list[Finding]:","+    \"\"\"Execute a single semantic check via LLM and parse response into Findings.","+","+    The factory should return an object with a run(prompt) method that","+    returns the LLM response string. Response is parsed as YAML.","+    \"\"\"","+    from .convergence import compute_stable_id","+","+    try:","+        process = claude_process_factory()","+        response_text = process.run(prompt)","+    except Exception as exc:","+        logger.error(\"Semantic check failed for %s: %s\", dimension, exc)","+        return []","+","+    # Parse YAML response","+    try:","+        parsed = yaml.safe_load(response_text)","+    except (yaml.YAMLError, AttributeError):","+        logger.warning(\"Failed to parse semantic response YAML for %s\", dimension)","+        return []","+","+    if not isinstance(parsed, dict):","+        return []","+","+    findings_data = parsed.get(\"findings\", [])","+    if not isinstance(findings_data, list):","+        return []","+","+    findings: list[Finding] = []","+    for entry in findings_data:","+        if not isinstance(entry, dict):","+            continue","+        description = entry.get(\"description\", \"\")","+        severity = entry.get(\"severity\", \"MEDIUM\")","+        location = entry.get(\"location\", f\"semantic:{dimension}\")","+        rule_id = f\"semantic_{dimension}\"","+        stable_id = compute_stable_id(dimension, rule_id, location, rule_id)","+","+        findings.append(Finding(","+            id=f\"{dimension}-semantic-{stable_id[:8]}\",","+            severity=severity,","+            dimension=dimension,","+            description=description,","+            location=location,","+            evidence=entry.get(\"evidence\", \"\"),","+            fix_guidance=entry.get(\"fix_guidance\", f\"Review {dimension} for semantic issues\"),","+            status=\"ACTIVE\",","+            source_layer=\"semantic\",","+            rule_id=rule_id,","+            spec_quote=entry.get(\"spec_quote\", \"\"),","+            roadmap_quote=entry.get(\"roadmap_quote\", \"\"),","+            stable_id=stable_id,","+        ))","+","+    return findings","+","+","+# --- T04.03: validate_semantic_high() Debate Protocol ---","+","+def validate_semantic_high(","+    finding: Finding,","+    registry: Any,  # DeviationRegistry","+    output_dir: Path,","+    *,","+    claude_process_factory: Callable | None = None,","+) -> str:","+    \"\"\"Orchestrate lightweight adversarial debate for a semantic HIGH finding.","+","+    Implements FR-4.1 protocol steps 1-7:","+    1. Build prosecutor + defender prompts from finding context","+    2. Execute both via ClaudeProcess in parallel (2 LLM calls)","+    3. Parse YAML responses; default scores to 0 on parse failure","+    4. Score both via score_argument() against 4-criterion rubric","+    5. Compute verdict via judge_verdict()","+    6. Write debate YAML to output_dir/debates/{stable_id}/debate.yaml","+    7. Call wire_debate_verdict() to update registry","+","+    Args:","+        finding: The semantic HIGH finding to validate.","+        registry: Active deviation registry for verdict recording.","+        output_dir: Directory for debate output artifacts.","+        claude_process_factory: Optional factory for ClaudeProcess creation.","+","+    Returns:","+        Verdict string: \"CONFIRM_HIGH\", \"DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM\", or \"DOWNGRADE_TO_LOW\".","+    \"\"\"","+    stable_id = getattr(finding, 'stable_id', '') or ''","+","+    # Step 1: Build prompts","+    prosecutor_prompt = PROSECUTOR_TEMPLATE.format(","+        dimension=finding.dimension,","+        rule_id=getattr(finding, 'rule_id', ''),","+        description=finding.description,","+        spec_quote=getattr(finding, 'spec_quote', ''),","+        roadmap_quote=getattr(finding, 'roadmap_quote', ''),","+        spec_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",","+        roadmap_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",","+    )","+    defender_prompt = DEFENDER_TEMPLATE.format(","+        dimension=finding.dimension,","+        rule_id=getattr(finding, 'rule_id', ''),","+        description=finding.description,","+        spec_quote=getattr(finding, 'spec_quote', ''),","+        roadmap_quote=getattr(finding, 'roadmap_quote', ''),","+        spec_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",","+        roadmap_context=\"[Semantic finding — see description]\",","+    )","+","+    # Step 2: Execute both in parallel","+    prosecutor_yaml: dict = {}","+    defender_yaml: dict = {}","+","+    if claude_process_factory is not None:","+        def _run_side(prompt: str) -> dict:","+            try:","+                process = claude_process_factory()","+                response = process.run(prompt)","+                parsed = yaml.safe_load(response)","+                return parsed if isinstance(parsed, dict) else {}","+            except Exception:","+                return {}","+","+        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2) as pool:","+            p_future = pool.submit(_run_side, prosecutor_prompt)","+            d_future = pool.submit(_run_side, defender_prompt)","+            prosecutor_yaml = p_future.result()","+            defender_yaml = d_future.result()","+","+    # Step 3 & 4: Score both sides (defaults to 0 on parse failure)","+    prosecutor_scores = score_argument(prosecutor_yaml)","+    defender_scores = score_argument(defender_yaml)","+","+    # Extract defender recommendation","+    defender_rec = defender_yaml.get(\"recommended_severity\", \"MEDIUM\")","+    if defender_rec not in (\"MEDIUM\", \"LOW\"):","+        defender_rec = \"MEDIUM\"","+","+    # Step 5: Deterministic verdict","+    verdict, margin = judge_verdict(prosecutor_scores, defender_scores, defender_rec)","+","+    # Step 6: Write debate YAML (T04.04)","+    debate_dir = output_dir / \"debates\" / stable_id","+    debate_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)","+    debate_yaml_path = debate_dir / \"debate.yaml\"","+","+    debate_output = {","+        \"finding_stable_id\": stable_id,","+        \"dimension\": finding.dimension,","+        \"description\": finding.description,","+        \"prosecutor\": {","+            \"evidence_quality\": prosecutor_scores.evidence_quality,","+            \"impact_specificity\": prosecutor_scores.impact_specificity,","+            \"logical_coherence\": prosecutor_scores.logical_coherence,","+            \"concession_handling\": prosecutor_scores.concession_handling,","+            \"weighted_score\": prosecutor_scores.weighted_score,","+        },","+        \"defender\": {","+            \"evidence_quality\": defender_scores.evidence_quality,","+            \"impact_specificity\": defender_scores.impact_specificity,","+            \"logical_coherence\": defender_scores.logical_coherence,","+            \"concession_handling\": defender_scores.concession_handling,","+            \"weighted_score\": defender_scores.weighted_score,","+        },","+        \"margin\": margin,","+        \"verdict\": verdict,","+    }","+","+    with open(debate_yaml_path, \"w\") as f:","+        yaml.dump(debate_output, f, default_flow_style=False, sort_keys=False)","+","+    # Step 7: Wire verdict into registry (T04.04)","+    wire_debate_verdict(registry, finding, verdict, str(debate_yaml_path))","+","+    logger.info(","+        \"Debate for %s: verdict=%s margin=%.3f\",","+        stable_id[:8], verdict, margin,","+    )","+","+    return verdict"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

\"\"\"validate_semantic_high() returns a verdict string.\"\"\"\n        # Strong prosecutor, weak defender -> CONFIRM_HIGH\n        prosecutor_resp = yaml.dump({\n            \"argument\": \" \"MEDIUM\",\n        })\n\n        call_count = [0]\n        def factory():\n            call_count[0] += 1\n            if call_count[0] % 2 == 1:\n                return FakeClaudeProcess(prosecutor_resp)\n            return FakeClaudeProcess(defender_resp)\n\n        finding = _make_test_finding(severity=\"HIGH\")\n        registry = _make_test_registry(tmp_path)\n claude_process_factory=factory,\n        )\n\n        assert verdict in (\"CONFIRM_HIGH\", \"DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM\", \"DOWNGRADE_TO_LOW\")\n\n    def scores.logical_coherence == 3\n        assert scores.concession_handling >= 2  # \"however\" + \"although\"\n\n\nclass TestJudgeVerdict:\n    \"\"\"BF-6: Deterministic verdict from rubric scores.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_clear_prosecutor_win(self):\n        \"\"\"Strong prosecutor -> CONFIRM_HIGH.\"\"\"\n        p = RubricScores(3, 3, 3, 3)\n        d = RubricScores(0, 0, 0, 0)\n        verdict, margin = judge_verdict(p, d)\n        assert verdict == \"CONFIRM_HIGH\"\n        assert margin > VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD\n\n    def test_clear_defender_win(self):\n        \"\"\"Strong defender -> DOWNGRADE.\"\"\"\n        p = RubricScores(0, 0, 0, 0)\n        d = RubricScores(3, 3, 3, 3)\n        verdict, margin = judge_verdict(p, d, defender_recommendation=\"MEDIUM\")\n        assert verdict == \"DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM\"\n        assert margin < -VERDICT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD\n\n    def test_tiebreak_favors_high(self):\n        \"\"\"Equal scores -> CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative).\"\"\"\n        p = RubricScores(2, 2, 2, 2)\n        d = RubricScores(2, 2, 2, 2)\n        verdict, margin = judge_verdict(p, d)\n        assert verdict == \"CONFIRM_HIGH\"\n\n\n# --- BF-7: Prompt budget enforcement ---\n\n\nclass TestPromptBudget:\n    \"\"\"BF-7: 30KB prompt size enforcement.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_truncate_within_budget(self):\n        \"\"\"Content within budget is unchanged.\"\"\"\n        text = \"Hello world\\n\"\n        result = _truncate_to_budget(text, 1000)\n        assert result == text\n\n    def test_truncate_oversized(self):\n        \"\"\"Oversized content is truncated with marker.\"\"\"\n        text = \"x\" * 50_000\n        result = _truncate_to_budget(text, 1000)\n        assert len(result.encode(\"utf-8\")) <= 1000\n        assert \"TRUNCATED\" in result\n\n    def test_truncate_on_line_boundary(self):\n        \"\"\"Truncation happens on line boundaries.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\"Line \" + str(i) for i in range(100)]\n        text = \"\\n\".join(lines)\n        result = _truncate_to_budget(text, 200)\n        # Should not end mid-line (before the marker)\n        before_marker = result.split(\"[TRUNCATED\")[0]\n        assert before_marker.endswith(\"\\n\") or before_marker == \"\"\n\n    def test_build_prompt_under_budget(self):\n        \"\"\"Normal prompt stays under 30KB.\"\"\"\n        request = heading\n        self.heading_path = heading\n        self.content = content\n\n\nclass FakeClaudeProcess:\n    \"\"\"Injected LLM stub returning canned YAML responses.\"\"\"\n    def __init__(self, response: str):\n        self._response = response\n\n    def run(self, prompt: str) -> str:\n        return self._response\n\n\ndef _make_test_finding(\n    dimension: str = \"prose_sufficiency\",\n    severity: str = \"HIGH\",\n    stable_id: str = \"abc123def456\",\n    source_layer: str = \"semantic\",\n    **kwargs,\n) -> Finding:\n    \"\"\"Create a test Finding with sensible defaults.\"\"\"\n    defaults = dict(\n        id=f\"{dimension}-test-{stable_id[:8]}\",\n        severity=severity,\n        dimension=dimension,\n        description=f\"Test finding in {dimension}\",\n        location=f\"spec:{dimension}\",\n        evidence=\"Test evidence\",\n        fix_guidance=\"Fix it\",\n        status=\"ACTIVE\",\n        source_layer=source_layer,\n        rule_id=f\"semantic_{dimension}\",\n        spec_quote=\"spec says X\",\n        roadmap_quote=\"roadmap says Y\",\n        stable_id=stable_id,\n    )\n    defaults.update(kwargs)\n    return Finding(**defaults)\n\n\ndef _make_test_registry(tmp_path: Path, findings: dict | None = None) -> DeviationRegistry:\n    \"\"\"Create a test DeviationRegistry with optional seeded findings.\"\"\"\n    registry_path = tmp_path / \"test-registry.json\"\n    reg = DeviationRegistry(\n        path=registry_path,\n        release_id=\"test-release\",\n        spec_hash=\"testhash123\",\n    )\n    if findings:\n        reg.findings = findings\n    return reg\n\n\n# --- T04.01: run_semantic_layer() ---\n\n\nclass TestDimensionFiltering:\n    \"\"\"T04.01: Semantic layer receives only non-structural dimensions.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_structural_dimensions_defined(self):\n        \"\"\"Structural dimensions match checker registry keys.\"\"\"\n        assert STRUCTURAL_DIMENSIONS == frozenset({\n            \"signatures\", \"data_models\", \"gates\", \"cli\", \"nfrs\",\n        })\n\n    def test_semantic_dimensions_disjoint_from_structural(self):\n        \"\"\"Semantic dimensions do not overlap with structural dimensions.\"\"\"\n        assert STRUCTURAL_DIMENSIONS.isdisjoint(SEMANTIC_DIMENSIONS)\n\n    def test_semantic_dimensions_non_empty(self):\n        \"\"\"At least one semantic dimension is defined.\"\"\"\n        assert len(SEMANTIC_DIMENSIONS) > 0\n\n    def test_run_semantic_layer_processes_only_semantic_dims(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"run_semantic_layer only processes SEMANTIC_DIMENSIONS, not structural.\"\"\"\n        called_prompts = []\n\n        def factory():\n            class Proc:\n                def run(self, prompt):\n                    called_prompts.append(prompt)\n                    return \"findings: []\"\n            return Proc()\n\n        registry = _make_test_registry(tmp_path)\n        registry.begin_run(\"roadmaphash\")\n\n        result = run_semantic_layer(\n            spec_sections=[FakeSection(\"S1\", \"content\")],\n            roadmap_sections=[FakeSection(\"R1\", \"content\")],\n            structural_findings=[],\n            registry=registry,\n            output_dir=tmp_path,\n            claude_process_factory=factory,\n        )\n\n        # Should have \"x\" * 2000\n        request = SemanticCheckRequest(\n            dimension=long_dim,\n            spec_sections=[],\n            roadmap_sections=[],\n            structural_findings=[],\n
