TestDebateProtocol:\n    \"\"\"T04.03: Adversarial debate for semantic HIGH validation.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_validate_semantic_high_returns_verdict(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"validate_semantic_high() returns a verdict string.\"\"\"\n        # Strong prosecutor, weak defender -> CONFIRM_HIGH\n        prosecutor_resp = yaml.dump({\n            \"argument\": \" \".join([\"evidence\"] * 120) + \" however despite\",\n            \"evidence_points\": [\"p1\", \"p2\", \"p3\"],\n            \"confidence\": 0.95,\n            \"recommended_severity\": \"HIGH\",\n        })\n        defender_resp = yaml.dump({\n            \"argument\": \"weak\",\n            \"evidence_points\": [],\n            \"confidence\": 0.1,\n            \"recommended_severity\": \"MEDIUM\",\n        })\n\n        call_count = [0]\n        def factory():\n            call_count[0] += 1\n            if call_count[0] % 2 == 1:\n                return FakeClaudeProcess(prosecutor_resp)\n            return FakeClaudeProcess(defender_resp)\n\n        finding = _make_test_finding(severity=\"HIGH\")\n        registry = _make_test_registry(tmp_path)\n        registry.begin_run(\"roadmaphash\")\n\n        verdict = validate_semantic_high(\n            finding=finding,\n            registry=registry,\n            output_dir=tmp_path,\n            claude_process_factory=factory,\n        )\n\n        assert verdict in (\"CONFIRM_HIGH\", \"DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM\", \"DOWNGRADE_TO_LOW\")\n\n    def test_deterministic_judge_same_inputs_same_verdict(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Same rubric scores always produce the same verdict (determinism).\"\"\"\n        # Both sides get same canned response\n        response = yaml.dump({\n            \"argument\": \" \".join([\"word\"] * 60) + \" however although\",\n            \"evidence_points\": [\"p1\", \"p2\"],\n            \"confidence\": 0.7,\n            \"recommended_severity\": \"MEDIUM\",\n        })\n\n        def factory():\n            return FakeClaudeProcess(response)\n\n        finding = _make_test_finding(severity=\"HIGH\")\n        registry1 = _make_test_registry(tmp_path / \"r1\")\n        registry1.begin_run(\"hash1\")\n        registry2 = _make_test_registry(tmp_path / \"r2\")\n        registry2.begin_run(\"hash2\")\n\n        v1 = validate_semantic_high(\n            finding=finding,\n            registry=registry1,\n            output_dir=tmp_path / \"out1\",\n            claude_process_factory=factory,\n        )\n        v2 = validate_semantic_high(\n            finding=finding,\n            registry=registry2,\n            output_dir=tmp_path / \"out2\",\n            claude_process_factory=factory,\n        )\n\n        assert v1 == v2  # Deterministic\n\n    def test_conservative_tiebreak_confirms_high(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Equal-strength arguments -> CONFIRM_HIGH (conservative tiebreak).\"\"\"\n        equal_resp = yaml.dump({\n            \"argument\": \" \".join([\"word\"] * 60) + \" however\",\n            \"evidence_points\": [\"p1\", \"p2\"],\n            \"confidence\": 0.7,\n            \"recommended_severity\": \"MEDIUM\",\n        })\n\n        def factory():\n            return FakeClaudeProcess(equal_resp)\n\n        finding = _make_test_finding(severity=\"HIGH\")\n        registry = _make_test_registry(tmp_path)\n        registry.begin_run(\"hash\")\n\n        verdict = validate_semantic_high(\n            finding=finding,\n            registry=registry,\n            output_dir=tmp_path,\n            claude_process_factory=factory,\n        )\n\n        assert verdict == \"CONFIRM_HIGH\"\n\n    def test_yaml_parse_failure_defaults_to_zero(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"YAML parse failure defaults all rubric scores to 0 for that side.\"\"\"\n        # Prosecutor returns garbage, defender returns valid\n        call_count = [0]\n        defender_resp = yaml.dump({\n            \"argument\": \" \".join([\"strong\"] * 120) + \" however despite although\",\n            \"evidence_points\": [\"p1\", \"p2\", \"p3\"],\n            \"confidence\": 0.95,\n            \"recommended_severity\": \"MEDIUM\",\n        })\n\n        def factory():\n            call_count[0] += 1\n            if call_count[0] % 2 == 1:\n                return FakeClaudeProcess(\"NOT VALID YAML {{{{\")\n            return FakeClaudeProcess(defender_resp)\n\n        finding = _make_test_finding(severity=\"HIGH\")\n        registry = _make_test_registry(tmp_path)\n        registry.begin_run(\"hash\")\n\n        verdict = validate_semantic_high(\n            finding=finding,\n            registry=registry,\n            output_dir=tmp_path,\n            claude_process_factory=factory,\n        )\n\n        # Prosecutor scores 0, strong defender should win\n        assert verdict == \"DOWNGRADE_TO_MEDIUM\"\n\n    def test_parallel_execution_both_sides(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Both prosecutor and defender execute (2 LLM calls).\"\"\"\n        call_count = [0]\n\n        def factory():\n            call_count[0] += 1\n            return FakeClaudeProcess(yaml.dump({\n                \"argument\": \"test argument\",\n                \"evidence_points\": [],\n                \"confidence\": 0.5,\n                \"recommended_severity\": \"MEDIUM\",\n            }))\n\n        finding = _make_test_finding(severity=\"HIGH\")\n        registry = _make_test_registry(tmp_path)\n        registry.begin_run(\"hash\")\n\n        validate_semantic_high(\n            finding=finding,\n            registry=registry,\n            output_dir=tmp_path,\n            claude_process_factory=factory,\n        )\n\n        assert call_count[0] == 2  # Prosecutor + Defender\n\n\n# --- T04.04: Debate YAML Output and Registry Wiring      \"\"\"Prior findings from registry appear in semantic layer prompt.\"\"\"\n        registry = _make_test_registry(tmp_path, findings={\n            \"prior1234567890ab\": {\n \"debate_verdict\": None,\n                \"debate_transcript\": None,\n            },\n        })\n        registry.begin_run(\"roadmaphash\")\n\n        # Semantic check returns empty — should NOT claude_process_factory=factory,\n        )\n\n        # No findings should reference the fixed ID\n        for f in result.findings:\n            assert f.stable_id != \"fixed123456789012\"\n\n    def test_prior_summary_within_budget_allocation(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Prior findings summary fits within 15% budget allocation (4608 bytes).\"\"\"\n        # Create 60 findings (above 50 limit) ====================================================================\n# T02.05 — Determinism Proof (SC-1)\n# ====================================================================\n\n\nclass TestDeterminism:\n    \"\"\"T02.05: SC-1 determinism proof — identical inputs produce byte-identical findings.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_sequential_determinism(self, spec_file: str, roadmap_file: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Two sequential runs on identical inputs produce identical findings.\"\"\"\n        findings_1 = run_all_checkers(spec_file, roadmap_file)\n        findings_2 = run_all_checkers(spec_file, roadmap_file)\n\n        assert len(findings_1) == len(findings_2), (\n            f\"Finding count differs: {len(findings_1)} vs {len(findings_2)}\"\n        )\n\n        for f1, f2 in zip(findings_1, findings_2):\n            assert f1.id == f2.id, f\"ID mismatch: {f1.id} vs {f2.id}\"\n            assert f1.severity == f2.severity\n            assert f1.dimension == f2.dimension\n            assert f1.rule_id == f2.rule_id\n            assert f1.location == f2.location\n            assert f1.spec_quote == f2.spec_quote\n            assert f1.roadmap_quote == f2.roadmap_quote\n            assert f1.stable_id == f2.stable_id\n\n    def test_serialized_output_identical(self, spec_file: str, roadmap_file: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Serialized output is byte-identical across runs.\"\"\"\n        import json\n\n        def serialize(findings: list) -> str:\n            return json.dumps(\n                [\n                    {\n                        \"id\": f.id,\n                        \"severity\": f.severity,\n                        \"dimension\": f.dimension,\n                        \"rule_id\": f.rule_id,\n                        \"location\": f.location,\n                        \"spec_quote\": f.spec_quote,\n                        \"roadmap_quote\": f.roadmap_quote,\n                        \"stable_id\": f.stable_id,\n                    }\n                    for f in findings\n                ],\n                sort_keys=True,\n                indent=2,\n            )\n\n        run1 = serialize(run_all_checkers(spec_file, roadmap_file))\n        run2 = serialize(run_all_checkers(spec_file, roadmap_file))\n        assert run1 == run2, \"Serialized output differs between runs\"\n\n    def test_individual_checker_determinism(self, spec_file: str, roadmap_file: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Each individual checker is deterministic.\"\"\"\n        for dim, checker in CHECKER_REGISTRY.items():\n            findings_1 = checker(spec_file, roadmap_file)\n            findings_2 = checker(spec_file, roadmap_file)\n            assert len(findings_1) == len(findings_2), f\"Checker '{dim}' non-deterministic count\"\n            for f1, f2 in zip(findings_1, findings_2):\n                assert f1.stable_id == f2.stable_id, f\"Checker '{dim}' non-deterministic stable_id\"\n\n    def test_parallel_execution_determinism(self, spec_file: str, roadmap_file: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Parallel execution produces same results as sequential (NFR-4).\"\"\"\n        from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor\n\n        sequential: list = []\n        for dim, checker in CHECKER_REGISTRY.items():\n            sequential.extend(checker(spec_file, roadmap_file))\n        sequential.sort(key=lambda f: (f.dimension, f.rule_id, f.location))\n\n        parallel: list = []\n        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=5) as pool:\n            futures = {\n                pool.submit(checker, spec_file, roadmap_file): dim\n                for dim, checker in CHECKER_REGISTRY.items()\n            }\n            for future in futures:\n                parallel.extend(future.result())\n        parallel.sort(key=lambda f: (f.dimension, f.rule_id, f.location))\n\n        assert len(sequential) == len(parallel)\n        for s, p in zip(sequential, parallel):\n            assert s.stable_id == p.stable_id\n            assert s.severity == p.severity\n            assert s.rule_id == p.rule_id\n\n    def test_no_shared_mutable_state(self,
